39

00:03:05,780  -->  00:03:09,800
Now that on its own, can make me think of so many ways to attack Zaid.

40

00:03:10,120  -->  00:03:13,240
We can look in at how active he is on Udemy.

41

00:03:13,540  -->  00:03:16,120
We can just pretend to be a person from Udemy,

42

00:03:16,150  -->  00:03:23,510
so we can be, we can pretend to be someone from the admins of Udemy and send him a program,

43

00:03:23,510  -->  00:03:29,440
for example, and tell him, "This is our new beta program that we're only giving to special instructors."

44

00:03:29,800  -->  00:03:34,540
This way Zaid will feel privileged because he's getting something that other instructors are not getting.

45

00:03:35,000  -->  00:03:36,740
And he'll run that file

46

00:03:37,140  -->  00:03:40,040
and once he runs that file, that file is going to be a Trojan.

47

00:03:40,540  -->  00:03:45,740
So that file can be a backdoor or a keylogger or a program that will steal his passwords

48

00:03:45,940  -->  00:03:49,020
or allow us to do anything we want on the target's computer.

49

00:03:49,540  -->  00:03:53,980
Now again, like I've said in this section, we're not going to be talking about the technical stuff,

50

00:03:53,980  -->  00:03:57,460
we'll talk about that in future lectures and you'll know how to do this.

51

00:03:57,920  -->  00:04:02,200
So when I say, we'll send him a file that looks like a normal file, we'll actually be able to do that

52

00:04:02,540  -->  00:04:07,940
and this normal file, it will be a normal program but at the background it will do what we wanted to do.

53

00:04:09,440  -->  00:04:14,420
We can also pretend to be from YouTube, from the blog, from WordPress,

54

00:04:14,740  -->  00:04:18,240
from Udemy again and ask him to reset their password

55

00:04:18,440  -->  00:04:21,980
and give them a link that has a log in page,

56

00:04:22,240  -->  00:04:25,940
exactly the same, as the log in page of Udemy or YouTube

57

00:04:26,080  -->  00:04:29,320
and once they put on their password, we'll actually get that password.

58

00:04:29,680  -->  00:04:31,440
So, the possibilities are endless there.

59

00:04:31,700  -->  00:04:35,220
And we'll talk about ideas on how to do all of that later on.

60

00:04:36,560  -->  00:04:40,220
Looking at the connections that we can see here with isecurity,

61

00:04:40,520  -->  00:04:44,680
we can see that this person has a lot of connections with this company or with this website.

62

00:04:45,200  -->  00:04:47,860
We can see that he has an email @isecurity.org

63

00:04:48,680  -->  00:04:53,640
So again, this email is really useful because this is how we're going to be communicating with Zaid.

64

00:04:54,080  -->  00:04:58,300
You can also communicate with him through Udemy, obviously as sending messages.

65

00:04:58,480  -->  00:05:03,760
but, I'm pretty sure that his friends and yes my friends, don't really communicate with me over Udemy.

66

00:05:05,400  -->  00:05:09,180
So, if you want to target Zaid using his email, that's another way to do it.

67

00:05:09,780  -->  00:05:13,558
And we have his friends, so we have one of his friends right here, Mohammad

68

00:05:13,820  -->  00:05:16,940
and you can see there's so many connections between him and Zaid.

69

00:05:17,160  -->  00:05:18,620
They're both friends on Twitter.

70

00:05:18,900  -->  00:05:24,780
They're both active on isecurity and they both have @isecurity.org email.

71

00:05:25,440  -->  00:05:30,100
So, this makes you think they're not only work colleagues, they're probably friends as well.

72

00:05:30,560  -->  00:05:32,460
And exploiting this friendship,

73

00:05:32,920  -->  00:05:36,200
you can send stuff to Zaid asking him about anything really.

74

00:05:36,320  -->  00:05:40,440
You can show him pictures of a car that you want to buy, for example,

75

00:05:40,640  -->  00:05:46,680
you can send him PDFs because you know that both of these guys are interested about computer security,

76

00:05:46,680  -->  00:05:48,660
if they're running a security website

77

00:05:48,900  -->  00:05:52,460
and you can just send him a PDF saying, "Oh, look at my new ebook."

78

00:05:53,020  -->  00:05:58,380
And when they open the PDF, again, it'll run a file that you wanted to run on the system

79

00:05:58,380  -->  00:06:01,140
that'll give you gain access on Zaid's system.

80

00:06:02,020  -->  00:06:05,540
Now again, when you send an email, you'll be able to send an email

81

00:06:05,540  -->  00:06:08,960
that looks exactly as if it's coming from Mohammad.

82

00:06:09,940  -->  00:06:12,180
You can do the same with Mu, with Mustafa.

83

00:06:12,460  -->  00:06:18,060
So, you can send stuff to Zaid, you can send him stuff that they're interested in,

84

00:06:18,280  -->  00:06:23,680
you can send him just pictures, you can send him links to ask him to log in and do something.

85

00:06:24,160  -->  00:06:28,120
The possibilities again are endless when it comes to social engineering.

86

00:06:29,040  -->  00:06:34,200
Not only that but let's say, you tried everything and you couldn't hack into Zaid's system.

87

00:06:34,660  -->  00:06:38,920
You tried to pretend to be all of these people, you tried all the ideas that you can think of

88

00:06:39,200  -->  00:06:41,080
and you couldn't reach Zaid's system.

89

00:06:41,680  -->  00:06:47,140
This is not the end of the world, what you could do is, you can actually try to hack into one of his friends.

90

00:06:47,560  -->  00:06:50,260
So, you can try to hack into Mohammad's computer.

91

00:06:50,520  -->  00:06:52,600
You can try to hack into Mustafa's computer.

92

00:06:53,040  -->  00:06:57,940
From there, try to get into their Facebook and then communicate with Zaid over Facebook

93

00:06:58,320  -->  00:06:59,760
from their Facebook account

94

00:06:59,760  -->  00:07:04,080
because you can't really send the message that looks, like it's coming from Mohammad on Facebook,

95

00:07:04,140  -->  00:07:05,720
we can only do that with emails.

96

00:07:06,000  -->  00:07:12,020
But again, you can hack into these guys Facebooks accounts and then try to hack into Zaid.

97

00:07:13,120  -->  00:07:18,140
Why not hack into their accounts, hack into isecurity because you know these people are admins,

98

00:07:18,140  -->  00:07:23,800
they have @isecurity emails and they're, even if you research their names, you'll see that they're admins.

99

00:07:24,520  -->  00:07:27,880
Why not hack into their computers, hack into isecurity

100

00:07:28,180  -->  00:07:30,370
and Zaid definitely browses isecurity.

101

00:07:30,370  -->  00:07:37,200
So, embed a backdoor in there or change one of the files that's hosted into isecurity into a backdoor

102

00:07:37,440  -->  00:07:42,160
and then once Zaid downloads it or uses it, you'll be able to hack into his computer.

103

00:07:44,380  -->  00:07:48,860
Now again, don't get overwhelmed about all the information that I'm saying at the moment.

104

00:07:49,080  -->  00:07:55,260
We will take this step by step and you'll learn how to send these fake emails, how to create these backdoors,

105

00:07:55,500  -->  00:08:00,200
how to create these keyloggers and all that cool stuff in the next sections of this course.

106

00:08:00,760  -->  00:08:03,960
For now, I just wanted to show you, how powerful Maltego is

107

00:08:04,300  -->  00:08:09,320
and how you can use it to gather information about anything really, like we started with just a name,

108

00:08:09,320  -->  00:08:14,960
literally we started with Zaid Sabih and we were able to gather information about his websites, his blog,

109

00:08:14,960  -->  00:08:16,960
his YouTube, his friends, his emails,

110

00:08:17,360  -->  00:08:19,880
anything, like we literally profiled the whole person.

111

00:08:20,460  -->  00:08:26,140
And again this person is a techie person, a person who is interested into information technology,

112

00:08:26,460  -->  00:08:28,440
so he's very careful with what to share

113

00:08:28,460  -->  00:08:33,280
but we were still able to gather enough information to build up an attack strategy.

114

00:08:33,560  -->  00:08:35,680
If you do this against a normal person,

115

00:08:36,120  -->  00:08:40,640
you'll be surprised about the amount of information that you'll be able to gather about them.
