79

00:06:01,520  -->  00:06:05,060
and I'm just gonna change its style and I'm gonna go on styles

80

00:06:05,720  -->  00:06:08,720
and set it to Special Container to look like a command.

81

00:06:08,960  -->  00:06:10,220
This looks nice.

82

00:06:10,940  -->  00:06:15,160
Now, you can also include pictures to have the picture or the logo of the company.

83

00:06:15,260  -->  00:06:16,680
You can style this more.

84

00:06:16,820  -->  00:06:21,420
You can craft your message to make it even more acceptable but this will do for our example.

85

00:06:22,500  -->  00:06:24,740
Now I'm ready to go. I'm gonna send this.

86

00:06:27,260  -->  00:06:28,640
I'm going to solve the CAPTCHA.

87

00:06:33,400  -->  00:06:34,920
And my message is sent now,

88

00:06:35,180  -->  00:06:36,620
so I'm gonna go to my target.

89

00:06:37,760  -->  00:06:42,760
Now, it would be a good idea to actually specify to your target to log in through SSH

90

00:06:42,760  -->  00:06:47,860
and execute this command, then maybe even give them instructions on how to log in through SSH,

91

00:06:48,320  -->  00:06:49,580
but for now, this will do.

92

00:06:49,720  -->  00:06:51,300
I'm just going to go to my gmail.

93

00:06:53,540  -->  00:06:58,160
And as you can see, we have a message
coming from Leaseweb and if we open that.

94

00:07:01,640  -->  00:07:07,580
You can see that the message is coming from support@nl.leastweb.com, so it's a-- their actual email.

95

00:07:08,340  -->  00:07:12,900
And the letter is telling us to please execute this command into our web server.

96

00:07:13,380  -->  00:07:17,240
Now, I'm actually going to execute this command on Ubuntu machine

97

00:07:17,320  -->  00:07:19,720
but we're just going to pretend that this is the web server,

98

00:07:20,520  -->  00:07:22,080
so we're going to copy this command.

99

00:07:23,020  -->  00:07:24,460
We're going to open our terminal.

100

00:07:28,100  -->  00:07:33,040
And before I execute this command, I'm just going to listen for incoming connections on my Kali machine.

101

00:07:35,580  -->  00:07:38,160
So we're going to do the same command that we have been doing before.

102

00:07:38,280  -->  00:07:43,900
So it's 'nc -vv -l -p 8080', to listen for incoming connections on 8080.

103

00:07:44,760  -->  00:07:47,960
Now, once the person execute this command,

104

00:07:48,660  -->  00:07:50,360
you'll see that they're getting a new terminal.

105

00:07:51,300  -->  00:07:54,060
And they're gonna think that they fix their TCP issue

106

00:07:54,180  -->  00:07:56,520
because you tell them that they have a problem with their 'tcp'.

107

00:07:57,520  -->  00:08:02,320
Once we come here, we'll see that we have full access to that computer.

108

00:08:02,680  -->  00:08:09,720
We can execute commands and do anything that we want on the target person using the 'Unix' command.

109

00:08:10,220  -->  00:08:12,840
Now in future lectures in, in the post exploitation,

110

00:08:13,060  -->  00:08:18,040
we'll talk about how we can convert this access to an Empire access and to a Meterpreter access

111

00:08:18,300  -->  00:08:22,640
and how we can use this access to do anything we want to the target computer.

112

00:08:22,820  -->  00:08:26,960
But basically, now we have full control over the target computer

113

00:08:27,440  -->  00:08:30,000
and we did this only by sending them an email.

114

00:08:30,460  -->  00:08:35,060
All we did is, we literally sent an email and we asked them to execute this command.

115

00:08:36,760  -->  00:08:41,180
Now like I said, this command works on all Unix based computers,

116

00:08:41,240  -->  00:08:45,520
so it's going to work, if you're targeting, if you want the person to run it on the web server.

117

00:08:46,020  -->  00:08:51,540
It's going to work, if you want the person to run this on their Linux computer, like I just did.

118

00:08:52,020  -->  00:08:56,320
And it'll work, if you want the person to run it on their Mac OS X

119

00:08:56,440  -->  00:09:00,900
because this com-- 'cause OS X uses Bash and this command works on Bash as well.

120

00:09:01,820  -->  00:09:07,740
If you're targeting a person with OS X, you can just make the email look as if it's coming from Apple Support.

121

00:09:08,200  -->  00:09:12,040
Tell them that there is a critical major issue that they need to fix

122

00:09:12,040  -->  00:09:13,920
and they can run this command to fix it.

123

00:09:14,340  -->  00:09:16,480
They run the command, you get a Reverse shell.

124

00:09:16,620  -->  00:09:21,640
They don't have to download anything, they don't have to click on any links, they don't have to install anything,

125

00:09:22,080  -->  00:09:27,120
all they have to do is literally copy this command, paste it and you'll get full access to their computer.

126

00:09:28,820  -->  00:09:32,960
Now, also notice that you can see this message is coming from Leaseweb

127

00:09:33,040  -->  00:09:39,060
and we can see their website in here and if we click on it, we'll actually go to their Google Plus account.

128

00:09:39,340  -->  00:09:41,740
So it looks very trustable.

129

00:09:42,140  -->  00:09:43,360
The person is going to trust it

130

00:09:43,660  -->  00:09:48,100
and if you're pretending to be Apple, then it'll actually have Apple logo in here.

131

00:09:48,180  -->  00:09:50,000
And again the person will trust it,

132

00:09:50,060  -->  00:09:54,140
they'll think that they're getting this letter or this message from Apple.

133

00:09:56,600  -->  00:10:00,700
It's things like this that show you how powerful social engineering is

134

00:10:00,760  -->  00:10:04,460
because you were not really executing or exploiting anything.

135

00:10:04,800  -->  00:10:09,700
We're literally just fooling the target person into running this on their computer.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1

00:00:00,740  -->  00:00:03,420
Now, in this lecture I'd like to show you another scenario

2

00:00:03,900  -->  00:00:07,780
and during this scenario, we're gonna cover another really useful tool

3

00:00:08,260  -->  00:00:11,480
that again, you can use it in so many other different scenarios.

4

00:00:12,900  -->  00:00:15,600
I'm just trying to show you different methods and ideas

5

00:00:15,880  -->  00:00:21,100
and then you can use this ideas in your own way to exploit your system or your target.

6

00:00:22,260  -->  00:00:25,120
Now, we know our target is isecurity.

7

00:00:25,420  -->  00:00:27,880
We know isecurity have their website hosted here

8

00:00:28,260  -->  00:00:33,160
and we know that this web server is managed by Leaseweb.

9

00:00:34,100  -->  00:00:38,520
When we go to the website of Leaseweb, there is something really cool on the top here,

10

00:00:38,700  -->  00:00:40,780
which is a customer portal login.

11

00:00:41,560  -->  00:00:45,280
Basically, from here the customers log in to their servers

12

00:00:45,760  -->  00:00:49,140
and from there they can log into the File Manager.

13

00:00:49,140  -->  00:00:51,040
They can change the FTP settings.

14

00:00:51,180  -->  00:00:56,200
They can pretty much control the whole server including all the websites that's installed on the server.

15

00:00:56,980  -->  00:01:00,200
So if we managed to get the email and the password for this

16

00:01:00,660  -->  00:01:03,200
then, we'll have the email and password for isecurity.

17

00:01:03,660  -->  00:01:07,120
Now this could be useful in any case, you want to think about it.

18

00:01:07,400  -->  00:01:11,680
If your target in one of the admins then, you can gain access to their website

19

00:01:11,680  -->  00:01:15,240
and put a backdoor in there and from there gain your access to them

20

00:01:15,600  -->  00:01:19,160
or if your target is the website itself then, using this method,

21

00:01:19,460  -->  00:01:22,020
you'll basically, just get access to the whole website.

22

00:01:23,540  -->  00:01:30,080
Now, what we're gonna do is we're gonna create a website that looks exactly like this login page

23

00:01:31,440  -->  00:01:33,880
and then we're gonna send that link to our target,

24

00:01:34,260  -->  00:01:38,500
once they log in we'll get the username and the password that they used.

25

00:01:39,080  -->  00:01:41,840
Now, you can use this method with any login page.

26

00:01:42,080  -->  00:01:44,060
So you can use it with Gmail login pages

27

00:01:44,180  -->  00:01:48,180
with Facebook, with Yahoo, with Twitter, with anything that you want really.

28

00:01:48,400  -->  00:01:50,220
I'm just gonna use this as an example.

29

00:01:51,240  -->  00:01:55,240
Now, the first thing that we wanna do is download this web page.

30

00:01:55,240  -->  00:01:58,720
So we want a web page that looks exactly like this one.

31

00:02:00,300  -->  00:02:05,020
To do that, we're going to use a Firefox plugin called Web Scrapbook.

32

00:02:06,000  -->  00:02:08,740
So I have its web page opened in here.

33

00:02:09,020  -->  00:02:11,860
I will include it in the resources of this lecture.

34

00:02:12,380  -->  00:02:17,600
Once you navigate here all you'll have to do is just click on add to Firefox.

35

00:02:19,540  -->  00:02:23,700
We're gonna click on Add and this will install it for me.

36

00:02:23,960  -->  00:02:30,160
So I'm gonna click on OK and as you can see we have the icon of the plug-in right here on the right.

37

00:02:31,860  -->  00:02:36,880
So now, all you have to do is go to the web page that you want to download.

38

00:02:36,880  -->  00:02:40,840
The web page that you want to clone or create a replica of.

39

00:02:41,280  -->  00:02:43,980
And in my case, it's this web page right here.

40

00:02:44,580  -->  00:02:47,380
We're gonna go to the icon of Scrapbook.

41

00:02:48,300  -->  00:02:50,300
We're gonna click on capture tab.

42

00:02:50,640  -->  00:02:53,900
Make sure you click on the option that says source in here

43

00:02:55,660  -->  00:02:58,820
and it's gonna allow me to download an archive.

44

00:02:59,640  -->  00:03:03,080
So, I'm gonna click on OK and this will go into my downloads

45

00:03:03,720  -->  00:03:12,220
but basically, what's happening right now is Scrapbook created an archive of all the files used on this page

46

00:03:12,220  -->  00:03:18,800
including the images, the HTML code, the styles, the JavaScript code

47

00:03:19,100  -->  00:03:23,000
and all the other resources needed by this web page.

48

00:03:24,700  -->  00:03:29,880
So usually if you just save this page using Firefox, it will only save the HTML

49

00:03:30,400  -->  00:03:37,480
but right now, we downloaded the whole page with all of the resources that it needs to look like this.

50

00:03:38,860  -->  00:03:41,420
So we stored this archive in my downloads.

51

00:03:41,680  -->  00:03:44,040
So I'm gonna go down to another desktop.

52

00:03:44,400  -->  00:03:50,020
I'm gonna open my File Manager and I'm gonna go to Downloads.

53

00:03:50,740  -->  00:03:56,319
Right here, we have the archive that contains all of the files used by the target web page.

54

00:03:57,020  -->  00:04:03,180
And I'm gonna right click this and click on extract here to uncompress this archive.

55

00:04:04,820  -->  00:04:13,300
I'm also going to rename this to LeaseWeb, just so we can use it easier in the future

56

00:04:14,000  -->  00:04:18,700
and if I go inside this directory you can see we have a number of files.

57

00:04:18,840  -->  00:04:25,500
So we have fonts, we have style files, we have, HTML files, we have JavaScript files,

58

00:04:26,500  -->  00:04:32,020
So basically, like I said, we have all of the resources needed for the web page to work.

59

00:04:33,520  -->  00:04:38,900
You'll usually have a file called 'index.html' or 'default.html'.

60

00:04:39,560  -->  00:04:43,480
This is the file that loads by default when you go to a website.

61

00:04:44,380  -->  00:04:52,620
So if I double click this file right now, you will see I'll get a website identical to the target website

62

00:04:53,300  -->  00:04:55,900
but if you look here on the top, on the URL bar,

63

00:04:56,560  -->  00:05:01,540
you'll see that this website is being loaded locally from my own computer.

64

00:05:02,660  -->  00:05:06,260
So it's being loaded from the directory that we just downloaded.

65

00:05:07,400  -->  00:05:11,100
So this is the fake or clone website that we just downloaded

66

00:05:11,380  -->  00:05:15,600
and right here we have the actual real website on this web.

67

00:05:17,140  -->  00:05:23,980
So as you can see the websites are very identical and can be used in so many scenarios.

68

00:05:24,760  -->  00:05:30,560
So in this lecture, I just wanted to show you how to clone a website and download it locally.

69

00:05:30,820  -->  00:05:34,740
So you can modify it and use it with different social engineering attacks.

70

00:05:35,320  -->  00:05:40,740
And in the next lectures, you'll see how you can use this in real attack scenarios.

71

00:05:42,260  -->  00:05:45,940
So in the future, I won't show you how to clone a website or make a replica.

72

00:05:46,440  -->  00:05:49,920
I'll assume that you know how to do this by following this lecture

73

00:05:50,700  -->  00:05:53,720
and you can do this to make replica of any website.

74

00:05:53,900  -->  00:05:56,380
It doesn't have to be a login page like this one.

75

00:05:56,740  -->  00:06:02,280
It could be any website that you want to use in a specific social engineering attack.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,500 --> 00:00:06,800
Now that we have the website ready and it looks a mirror image of the original website, we are ready

2
00:00:06,800 --> 00:00:13,940
to go and try to modify the website that we downloaded so that it reports to us the things that the

3
00:00:13,940 --> 00:00:18,380
people type in the input fields in here and the username and in the password.

4
00:00:18,890 --> 00:00:23,270
To do that, we're going to use a tool called social Engineering toolkit.

5
00:00:23,300 --> 00:00:24,950
So I'm just going to close this.

6
00:00:26,450 --> 00:00:30,560
The social engineering toolkit is pre-installed in Kali Linux.

7
00:00:30,560 --> 00:00:35,000
So all you have to do is just type in set toolkit to run it.

8
00:00:35,990 --> 00:00:41,600
Now to navigate through this, you have to put the number beside the category that you want to go to.

9
00:00:41,870 --> 00:00:45,260
So we want to go and do a social engineering attack.

10
00:00:45,260 --> 00:00:46,730
So we're going to put number one.

11
00:00:47,700 --> 00:00:51,030
Then we want to go to the website attack vectors.

12
00:00:51,030 --> 00:00:52,560
So this is number two.

13
00:00:55,170 --> 00:00:58,200
And then we want to use the credential harvester.

14
00:00:58,200 --> 00:01:05,010
So this is the plugin that will inject code in the website that we just downloaded so that it reports

15
00:01:05,010 --> 00:01:08,310
everything that the people put on that website to us.

16
00:01:08,520 --> 00:01:10,290
So I'm going to put number three.

17
00:01:13,020 --> 00:01:21,810
And in here you can see that set toolkit actually gives us a number of options to create a website so

18
00:01:21,810 --> 00:01:23,460
we can use web templates.

19
00:01:23,460 --> 00:01:29,730
And in here you'll see already websites for famous websites like Google login page, Facebook login

20
00:01:29,730 --> 00:01:31,590
page, A, Yahoo login page.

21
00:01:31,590 --> 00:01:34,440
So you won't even have to do all the steps that I showed you.

22
00:01:35,270 --> 00:01:41,840
You can also put number two to use the site cloner and this will literally allow you to put a URL and

23
00:01:41,840 --> 00:01:47,060
then it will download the website automatically and inject the reporting code in it.

24
00:01:47,300 --> 00:01:51,770
So instead of what we did manually, it will just download everything for you automatically.

25
00:01:51,800 --> 00:01:57,260
The only problem is with my experience, this doesn't work against all websites and it actually wouldn't

26
00:01:57,260 --> 00:01:59,540
work against the website that I'm showing you now.

27
00:01:59,990 --> 00:02:05,720
So the best option is to manually download the website like I showed you and then use number three to

28
00:02:05,720 --> 00:02:11,900
use a custom import where you give the website to set and set will only inject the code that will give

29
00:02:11,900 --> 00:02:14,090
you reports of the input.

30
00:02:14,910 --> 00:02:16,620
So I'm going to put number three.

31
00:02:18,600 --> 00:02:21,780
And then it's asking us to enter our IP address.

32
00:02:21,810 --> 00:02:28,830
Now my IP address is 1020 14 to 13 and we covered before how to get your IP address using the ifconfig

33
00:02:28,830 --> 00:02:29,550
command.

34
00:02:29,730 --> 00:02:31,350
So I'm going to input that.

35
00:02:32,540 --> 00:02:40,400
Then it's asking me for the location where my website is stored and we start our website in.

36
00:02:40,670 --> 00:02:47,690
If we just go to the file manager here, you'll see it stored in home, which is root and then downloads,

37
00:02:47,690 --> 00:02:49,640
and then it's called Leaseweb.

38
00:02:50,740 --> 00:02:55,630
So we're going to just put the full path in here, which is route.

39
00:02:56,440 --> 00:02:57,490
Downloads.

40
00:02:58,350 --> 00:02:59,160
Leaseweb.

41
00:03:01,580 --> 00:03:05,810
And make sure to put a final forward slash at the end.

42
00:03:06,020 --> 00:03:07,610
Then we're going to hit enter.

43
00:03:09,100 --> 00:03:15,820
And it's going to ask me, do I want to only copy the index dot HTML or copy the entire folder and we

44
00:03:15,820 --> 00:03:21,010
want to copy the entire folder so that all the images and the fonts and the styles will be copied as

45
00:03:21,010 --> 00:03:21,550
well.

46
00:03:22,430 --> 00:03:26,900
Now, the last thing that it's going to ask you is the URL of the website that we imported.

47
00:03:26,900 --> 00:03:30,140
This is the URL for the original website.

48
00:03:30,170 --> 00:03:36,470
And in here I already went back and this is the URL, so I'm going to copy that.

49
00:03:38,110 --> 00:03:39,430
And paste it here.

50
00:03:41,000 --> 00:03:41,620
And that's it.

51
00:03:41,630 --> 00:03:42,560
We're good to go.

52
00:03:42,590 --> 00:03:48,980
Now, if anyone goes to 10 to 20 14 to 13, they'll see a website that is a mirror image of this and

53
00:03:48,980 --> 00:03:52,310
it's going to report anything that the people enter to us.

54
00:03:52,640 --> 00:03:56,000
Now, let's test this website before we send it to the target person.

55
00:03:56,000 --> 00:03:58,940
So we're just going to go to 10 to 20 14 to 13.

56
00:03:59,570 --> 00:04:04,970
And as you can see, we get a website that looks exactly like the target website.

57
00:04:04,970 --> 00:04:10,460
So we have the same logo, the same links in here, and these links are actually functional.

58
00:04:10,460 --> 00:04:14,840
So if you click on this, it'll actually take you where you're supposed to go.

59
00:04:17,180 --> 00:04:23,090
Now let's take this URL and go to our favorite URL shortening service.

60
00:04:23,540 --> 00:04:27,260
Put the URL here and we'll just put a nice name on it.

61
00:04:27,260 --> 00:04:29,510
So we'll say admin login.

62
00:04:31,470 --> 00:04:32,610
We'll shorten this.

63
00:04:33,640 --> 00:04:36,070
So we have a nice URL in here.

64
00:04:37,010 --> 00:04:44,150
And then we're going to go to our mailer to try to social engineer our target into opening our link

65
00:04:44,150 --> 00:04:45,980
and then logging into their website.

66
00:04:47,150 --> 00:04:51,050
So we're going to put the name as support like we did before.

67
00:04:52,480 --> 00:05:00,010
And then we're going to set the email from to the email of the support, at least Web.com, because

68
00:05:00,010 --> 00:05:03,910
this is the support team that's handling security.

69
00:05:03,940 --> 00:05:09,080
We managed to get that from Maltego when we were doing our information gathering.

70
00:05:09,100 --> 00:05:14,440
And then we're sending this to say that I security.org which again we got it from information gathering

71
00:05:14,440 --> 00:05:17,380
and we know that that person is an admin on that website.

72
00:05:18,970 --> 00:05:23,740
Now the subject again, you want to pick something that's going to make the target person open this

73
00:05:23,740 --> 00:05:25,600
message and do what's inside it.

74
00:05:25,720 --> 00:05:28,630
We're just going to say, please log in to your account.

75
00:05:30,520 --> 00:05:32,860
So we're going to say D or Z.

76
00:05:33,610 --> 00:05:35,080
We at Leaseweb.

77
00:05:36,470 --> 00:05:41,660
Are glad to inform you that we moved your account to a faster server.

78
00:05:42,680 --> 00:05:47,270
The new account can still be used exactly the same way as the old one.

79
00:05:47,840 --> 00:05:52,640
The only difference is your website and your services are going to function faster.

80
00:05:54,500 --> 00:05:57,680
Please log in here to activate the new account.

81
00:06:00,370 --> 00:06:03,100
Sincerely Leaseweb support.

82
00:06:04,450 --> 00:06:08,650
Now what you want to do is also put the link that we just created.

83
00:06:09,280 --> 00:06:15,790
I think it will be even better if we can make this word right here the here to be clickable.

84
00:06:15,790 --> 00:06:19,510
So when they click on it, they go to the link that we just created.

85
00:06:21,250 --> 00:06:23,860
That's actually very simple using HTML.

86
00:06:23,860 --> 00:06:28,600
And as you can see, the message supports HTML in here.

87
00:06:28,600 --> 00:06:33,410
So all we have to do is use the A tag in HTML.

88
00:06:33,430 --> 00:06:34,540
It's very simple.

89
00:06:34,540 --> 00:06:37,840
Even if you don't know how to do it, I'm going to show you how to do it right now.

90
00:06:37,840 --> 00:06:43,820
So all you need to do is use the greater and smaller size to create a new tag.

91
00:06:43,840 --> 00:06:52,360
This is going to be an A tag to specify a hyperlink and the link that we want the person to go to when

92
00:06:52,360 --> 00:06:59,500
they click on this word right here will be specified after the href argument.

93
00:06:59,530 --> 00:07:06,740
So we're going to say href is equal to my link and I'm going to enclose my link between two quotation

94
00:07:06,740 --> 00:07:07,370
marks.

95
00:07:08,760 --> 00:07:11,940
Let me just expand this so it's easier to see.

96
00:07:11,970 --> 00:07:19,590
So all we're doing is we're doing a ref followed by the link that we want the user to go to when they

97
00:07:19,590 --> 00:07:20,940
click on the link.

98
00:07:22,850 --> 00:07:24,740
Then we will close this.

99
00:07:24,740 --> 00:07:33,020
So we're just going to do smaller sign slash a greater sign, and this will basically close the link

100
00:07:33,020 --> 00:07:37,220
so that the only word that is linked right now is the here.

101
00:07:37,220 --> 00:07:41,060
So the here will be hyperlinked, it will be blue, it'll have the underline.

102
00:07:41,060 --> 00:07:47,840
And when someone clicks on it, they will go to the link that we specify in here in the href argument.

103
00:07:48,380 --> 00:07:54,170
So you can replace this link with any link that you want and this is a bit of HTML for you that will

104
00:07:54,170 --> 00:07:56,390
definitely be useful in the future.

105
00:07:58,300 --> 00:07:59,150
And that's it.

106
00:07:59,170 --> 00:08:00,850
Our message is ready to go.

107
00:08:00,850 --> 00:08:01,000
Now.

108
00:08:01,000 --> 00:08:04,360
You want to spell check this, make sure it's nice and formal.

109
00:08:04,360 --> 00:08:08,560
Make sure that the target person is interested into the service that you're giving them.

110
00:08:08,560 --> 00:08:11,680
And once you're satisfied with it, hit the send button.

111
00:08:13,100 --> 00:08:13,940
And that's it.

112
00:08:13,940 --> 00:08:15,440
The message is sent.

113
00:08:15,620 --> 00:08:22,370
Now, if we go down here, whenever the target person logs in and puts info, the info will be logged

114
00:08:22,370 --> 00:08:25,190
and displayed in here in the terminal window.

115
00:08:25,640 --> 00:08:27,710
Now let's go to the windows machine.

116

Go to gmail.com.

117

So this is Zaid now checking his email account.

118
00:08:34,470 --> 00:08:40,440
He is going to see a letter coming from Leaseweb support coming into his normal inbox and saying Please

119

log in to your account.

120

Z is an admin of a website.

121
00:08:43,470 --> 00:08:44,460
He is going to go in.

122

He's going to see the letter is coming from support at Leaseweb.

123
00:08:48,060 --> 00:08:49,050
So that's good.

124

He's going to see the Leaseweb profile in here and if he clicks on it, he'll actually go to their Google+

125
00:08:54,270 --> 00:08:54,690
page.

126
00:08:54,690 --> 00:09:00,090

127
00:09:00,090 --> 00:09:03,180
just log in to through this new link.

128
00:09:03,180 --> 00:09:06,840
And once he does that, he's going to get faster Internet on his website.

129
00:09:07,260 --> 00:09:13,020
Now we're going to put the username, which is, let's say Z that I security.org.

130
00:09:15,960 --> 00:09:21,480
And then let's say we're going to put the password and let's say the password is one, two, three,

131
00:09:21,480 --> 00:09:22,560
four, five, six.

132
00:09:24,050 --> 00:09:24,740
Password.

133
00:09:25,070 --> 00:09:26,420
Now we're going to hit Enter.

134
00:09:28,900 --> 00:09:35,980
The person will actually get redirected to the proper website that we entered in set toolkit.

135
00:09:35,980 --> 00:09:38,700
So they're actually going to go back to the customer portal.

136
00:09:38,710 --> 00:09:42,640
They'll think that they put a wrong password so they won't get very suspicious.

137
00:09:42,880 --> 00:09:50,440
But if we go back here, you'll see that we managed to capture their username, which is ZTE Security.org,

138
00:09:50,440 --> 00:09:54,940
and we managed to capture their password, which is one, two, three, four, five, six password,

139

and I misspelled password, but you get the idea.

140
00:09:59,250 --> 00:10:05,910
So you can use this method to mirror any website, any login page that you can think of, and then social

141
00:10:05,910 --> 00:10:08,820
engineering your target to log in once they log in.

You'll get the username and password and like I said, you can use this, you can try to get access

143

You're only limited by your imagination with this.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1

00:00:00,480  -->  00:00:05,220
In this lecture I'd like to show you a
really cool framework called 'BeEF Framework'.

2

00:00:06,000  -->  00:00:13,160
BeEF is a browser exploitation framework that allow us to run a number of commands on hooked browsers.

3

00:00:14,360  -->  00:00:16,360
Browsers can be hooked to BeEF,

4

00:00:16,480  -->  00:00:21,280
using a number of methods such as 'man-in-the-middle attack' and 'XSS vulnerabilities',

5

00:00:21,660  -->  00:00:26,900
but we're gonna focus on hooking people using a link, so as soon as they click on a link

6

00:00:27,120  -->  00:00:28,680
we'll have them hooked to BeEF.

7

00:00:28,960  -->  00:00:35,240
And once they're hooked to BeEF, we'll be able to run a large number of commands that BeEF allow us to run.

8

00:00:35,840  -->  00:00:40,360
This commands will allow us to deliver evil files and smart convincing ways.

9

00:00:40,720  -->  00:00:48,660
It will allow us to show fake updates, fake logins, take screenshots, inject Keyloggers and much more.

10

00:00:50,320  -->  00:00:54,180
BeEF uses JavaScript, so it works against all browsers

11

00:00:54,400  -->  00:00:58,820
that includes Firefox, Chrome, Internet Explorer and more.

12

00:00:59,200  -->  00:01:05,080
Pretty much all browsers, these days use JavaScript unless the user manually disables it.

13

00:01:05,640  -->  00:01:11,960
BeEF is actually going to be like a link between delivery methods and post exploitation

14

00:01:12,260  -->  00:01:18,260
because once a person is hooked to BeEF, you actually have a very high chance of fully exploiting their system.

15

00:01:18,760  -->  00:01:23,320
But as long as they're only hooked to BeEF, you don't have full access to their computer,

16

00:01:23,320  -->  00:01:26,520
you can only run the commands that BeEF allow you to do

17

00:01:26,860  -->  00:01:30,840
but we allow you to do a lot of things and we'll see how we can leverage BeEF,

18

00:01:31,300  -->  00:01:36,760
to get the target person to run something that'll allow us to gain full access to their computer.

19

00:01:38,020  -->  00:01:43,120
Now, in this lecture I'm just going to show you an overview and how to use a basic hook method

20

00:01:43,360  -->  00:01:47,120
and in the future we'll be using BeEF more and you'll get more comfortable with it.

21

00:01:48,120  -->  00:01:51,540
Now to run BeEF, we're just gonna click on its icon in here

22

00:01:51,800  -->  00:01:55,760
and that will automatically start the web browser, once BeEF loads.

23

00:01:59,580  -->  00:02:03,040
Now, the username is 'beef' and the password is 'beef' as well.

24

00:02:07,160  -->  00:02:10,920
Once you're logged in, you can see we
have the main user interface for BeEF,

25

00:02:11,240  -->  00:02:12,700
we'll talk about that later,

26

00:02:13,060  -->  00:02:16,520
but let's see how we can hook a target using a very basic hook method.

27

00:02:17,660  -->  00:02:19,440
So, I'm just gonna put this to the side.

28

00:02:20,380  -->  00:02:25,420
And I'm gonna copy the code in here, so it's actually giving us an example code that we can use,

29

00:02:25,940  -->  00:02:27,300
so I'm gonna copy this.

30

00:02:29,420  -->  00:02:33,780
And I'm gonna go to the location where I store my data, my Webroot is,

31

00:02:34,040  -->  00:02:36,700
which is '/var/www/html/'.

32

00:02:38,720  -->  00:02:41,760
Now, in here you can see my evil files which is the directory,

that contains all the backdoors that we've been delivering

and you can see a web page called 'index.html'.

35

Now, this is the default web page that gets loaded when people go to my IP address,

36

00:02:53,880  -->  00:02:58,480
when they try to browse my web browser, which is at '10.20.14.213.'

37

00:02:59,500  -->  00:03:02,700
So, I'm going to modify the code for this web page, so I'm going to right-click it

38

00:03:03,160  -->  00:03:05,680
and open it with my Text Editor.

44

00:03:29,660  -->  00:03:30,860
and quit it,

45

00:03:31,780  -->  00:03:32,760
and we're good to go.

46

00:03:33,100  -->  00:03:35,160
Now, I'm also gonna start my web server,

47

00:03:35,440  -->  00:03:36,720
the 'Apache web server',

48

00:03:36,720  -->  00:03:40,920
so I'm just gonna do 'service apache2 start'.

49

00:03:43,680  -->  00:03:46,000
And that's it. Now, that's, everything is perfect.

50

00:03:46,400  -->  00:03:50,240
Now, if people go to my web browser which is at 10.20.14.213,

51

00:03:50,600  -->  00:03:53,620
the default index web page will load which is this one.

52

00:03:54,040  -->  00:03:57,180
And once that loads, it has the hook code for BeEF

53

00:03:57,800  -->  00:04:00,120
and it's gonna hook that person to BeEF.

54

00:04:01,100  -->  00:04:06,120
Now like I said, this is a JavaScript code, so it doesn't matter where the person is gonna load that code on.

55

00:04:06,480  -->  00:04:11,940
If they open it through an android phone, if they open it through Mac OS, if they open it through Linux, Windows,

56

00:04:12,300  -->  00:04:16,300
it's gonna work on all browsers because all browser support JavaScript.

57

00:04:18,100  -->  00:04:24,780
So, I'm in here and I'm gonna open my website in Kali which is at '10.20.14.213',

58

00:04:25,400  -->  00:04:26,200
hit Enter

59

00:04:26,580  -->  00:04:29,240
and you'll see nothing loads because the webpage was empty.

60

00:04:30,040  -->  00:04:31,200
But if we come here,

61

00:04:31,780  -->  00:04:36,320
you'll see, we have a new browser showing up in the online browsers in here on the left.

62

00:04:37,480  -->  00:04:39,840
Now, you can see the IP of the machine.

63

00:04:40,320  -->  00:04:43,660
We can see it has a Windows logo, so we know this is a Windows machine.

64

00:04:44,600  -->  00:04:48,240
We can see that it's a Mozilla Firefox that the target person is using,

65

00:04:48,600  -->  00:04:50,640
it's Mozilla Firefox 52.

66

00:04:51,540  -->  00:04:55,660
You can see the plugins that's used, you can see that they don't have Flash installed.

67

00:04:56,000  -->  00:04:59,420
They don't have 'VBScript' installed,

68

00:05:00,120  -->  00:05:01,860
they have no QuickTime for example,

69

00:05:03,860  -->  00:05:05,140
the youth cookies.

70

00:05:06,440  -->  00:05:12,480
You can also see the page that they got hooked from, so they got hooked from my own web server,

71

00:05:12,940  -->  00:05:16,300
and in here, we can see information about the host itself.

72

00:05:16,560  -->  00:05:18,300
So, we can see its IP address,

73

00:05:18,620  -->  00:05:21,040
we can see its operating system which is Windows.

74

00:05:21,420  -->  00:05:24,660
We can see the architecture, it's a 64-bit computer.

75

00:05:26,180  -->  00:05:28,380
So, these information can all be handy.

76

00:05:28,540  -->  00:05:31,017
Once we come to exploiting the target like I said,

77

00:05:31,017  -->  00:05:34,040
the information gathering is one of the most important steps.

78

00:05:35,720  -->  00:05:40,360
Now, the tabs in here will allow you to navigate through the features that BeEF gives you.

79

00:05:41,020  -->  00:05:47,160
So, we can see the logs in the 'Logs tab' and you can see that we managed to hook a target in here.

80

00:05:47,360  -->  00:05:49,900
You can see when the window gets in focus and such.

81

00:05:50,840  -->  00:05:52,720
If we, if you click on commands,

82

00:05:53,240  -->  00:05:56,160
you'll see the place where we'll be doing most of the work,

83

00:05:56,620  -->  00:06:00,780
this is where you can run the cool commands that BeEF allow you to run

84

00:06:01,120  -->  00:06:03,620
and we'll get back to that in future lectures.

85

00:06:07,120  -->  00:06:11,140
The writer will allow you to see and create HTTP requests.

86

00:06:12,280  -->  00:06:17,240
XSS Rays will allow you to discover XSS vulnerabilities in the current page.

87

00:06:18,820  -->  00:06:23,560
IPEC will allow you to run BeEF commands from a BeEF terminal.

88

00:06:24,660  -->  00:06:28,320
In here, the network will give you an overview of the current network

89

00:06:28,860  -->  00:06:31,340
and we can see that we're running BeEF in here.

90

00:06:31,680  -->  00:06:33,560
We have the hooked browser in here

91

00:06:34,240  -->  00:06:37,880
and that hooked browser is installed on a Windows computer.

92

00:06:39,700  -->  00:06:47,680
And in the last tab, we have our web RTC which can be used to stay anonymous andmake it harder to track us.

93

00:06:49,940  -->  00:06:55,120
Now, in this lecture I just wanted to show you an overview of BeEF and a very basic hook method.

94

00:06:55,380  -->  00:06:57,860
We'll see in future videos better hook methods

00:04:13,546  -->  00:04:16,267
Once they click on that link, they'll be hooked to BeEF

50

00:04:16,267  -->  00:04:19,880
and then you'll be able to do all the attacks that BeEF allow us to do.

51

00:04:20,380  -->  00:04:22,920
Now I know we haven't seen anything that BeEF can do

52

00:04:23,020  -->  00:04:28,860
but you'll see in the future that BeEF can be very, very handy and it can be used to do so many cool things.

53

00:04:30,200  -->  00:04:35,740
Now I'm not gonna show you how to send the BeEF link to the target because it's very easy,

54

00:04:35,980  -->  00:04:40,940
you can just copy this link go to the URL shortening service, that I showed you.

55

00:04:40,940  -->  00:04:47,220
Shorten the URL and craft a nice social engineering letter to get the target to be hooked to BeEF.

56

00:04:47,620  -->  00:04:52,960
What I'm gonna show you though, a much cooler way to get your target to click on the-- On a link.

57

00:04:53,380  -->  00:04:57,400
So stay tuned for the next lecture and you'll see what I'm talking about.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
00:00:00,050 --> 00:00:08,210
Previously we learned how to use URL shortening services such as Bidu to transform our suspicious looking

2
00:00:08,240 --> 00:00:15,770
URLs such as this one right here to a less suspicious one to increase the chances of our target clicking

3
00:00:15,770 --> 00:00:18,110
on it and to use these services.

4
00:00:18,110 --> 00:00:22,610
All we had to do is simply copy the URL that we want to take the target to.

5
00:00:22,640 --> 00:00:28,550
So in this example, we're taking them to a fake clone of my own old blog.

6
00:00:28,580 --> 00:00:31,420
So we're going to simply paste the URL here.

7
00:00:31,430 --> 00:00:36,950
You could customize the URL by specifying what comes after the forward slash in here.

8
00:00:36,950 --> 00:00:43,280
So you could type something like test 22, whatever, verify that you're not a robot and click on Shorten.

9
00:00:43,280 --> 00:00:50,060
And when we do that, we're going to get a URL that will simply say bit dot do forward slash test 2

10
00:00:50,060 --> 00:00:50,790
to 2.

11
00:00:50,810 --> 00:00:57,830
But when the target clicks on it or puts it in their URL bar, they'll actually end up at our fake page.

12
00:00:58,040 --> 00:01:04,440
Therefore they'll be hooked to be if we had beef hook code embedded in this page or if it's a login

13
00:01:04,440 --> 00:01:08,970
page, they will be more likely to put their username and password in it.

14
00:01:09,390 --> 00:01:17,400
Now in this lecture I want to show you more tricks that we can use to make our URLs look less suspicious.

15
00:01:17,550 --> 00:01:22,890
So I'm going to start easy with another URL shortening service called tiny URL.

16
00:01:22,920 --> 00:01:26,820
You probably heard of it and using this is very similar to Bidu.

17
00:01:26,850 --> 00:01:29,700
There's actually a lot of URL shortening services.

18
00:01:29,700 --> 00:01:33,420
You should have a look on them, compare them and see which one you like the most.

19
00:01:33,450 --> 00:01:35,640
Let me show you why I like this one too.

20
00:01:35,640 --> 00:01:39,720
So we're simply going to put the URL again that we want to take the target to.

21
00:01:39,750 --> 00:01:46,560
You can actually specify a number of different domains in here, but the only free one is tiny URL,

22
00:01:46,590 --> 00:01:48,270
so we're going to keep it at that.

23
00:01:48,270 --> 00:01:53,220
And you could also specify the alias similar to what we did with Bidu.

24
00:01:53,250 --> 00:02:00,300
Now since this takes us to my or to a clone of my blog, I'm actually going to set this to the name

25
00:02:00,300 --> 00:02:01,290
of my blog.

26
00:02:01,290 --> 00:02:04,590
So ZA1D followed by blog.

27
00:02:04,920 --> 00:02:08,580
We're going to click on make tiny URL and perfect.

28
00:02:08,610 --> 00:02:12,360
Now we have a cool looking URL that is much less suspicious.

29
00:02:12,360 --> 00:02:17,810
So instead of giving the target this URL, we're going to be giving them this URL and actually says

30
00:02:17,820 --> 00:02:19,650
Zayed's blog at the back.

31
00:02:19,650 --> 00:02:23,580
And so if we copy this and paste it in our browser.

32
00:02:24,340 --> 00:02:28,790
You'll see we're actually going to end up at the fake cloned website.

33
00:02:28,810 --> 00:02:30,310
So that's pretty cool.

34
00:02:30,310 --> 00:02:32,520
But we've already done similar stuff.

35
00:02:32,530 --> 00:02:34,960
This just looks a little bit less suspicious.

36
00:02:34,960 --> 00:02:43,150
But what you could also do with tiny URL is download a QR code that you could scan with your mobile

37
00:02:43,150 --> 00:02:48,970
phone, and when you do that, you will actually end up at the website that we set as the target website

38
00:02:48,970 --> 00:02:49,660
in here.

39
00:02:50,050 --> 00:02:53,890
So let me show you the QR code actually looks very, very simple.

40
00:02:54,790 --> 00:03:00,790
So when this is scanned, it will actually take the user to the URL that we set in here.

41
00:03:01,150 --> 00:03:05,100
This could be very useful these days, especially after Covid.

42
00:03:05,110 --> 00:03:09,850
People are actually used now to scan these QR codes to see restaurant menus.

43
00:03:09,850 --> 00:03:11,800
It's actually used everywhere these days.

44
00:03:11,800 --> 00:03:13,000
So you could do that.

45
00:03:13,000 --> 00:03:17,680
And then when they scan this, they could actually land at a clone of a restaurant's menu.

46
00:03:17,680 --> 00:03:24,130
But in the code, you could have a beef hook code or you could ask them to log in or you could do whatever

47
00:03:24,130 --> 00:03:25,150
you learned so far.

48
00:03:25,150 --> 00:03:27,460
So you could actually use this with any other method.

49
00:03:27,460 --> 00:03:28,030
We learned.

50
00:03:28,060 --> 00:03:33,250
I'm just simply showing you tricks in this video, and using them is completely up to you.

00:03:33,920 --> 00:03:39,500
We could also use another cool trick to make this URL look even less suspicious.

52
00:03:39,530 --> 00:03:40,880
Let me show you what I mean.

53
00:03:40,880 --> 00:03:45,200
So I'm going to copy it and I'm just going to paste it here in my text editor.

54
00:03:45,200 --> 00:03:55,670
And we know that the legit, the real domain of my website, of my blog is actually Z, a1d.com.

55
00:03:56,440 --> 00:04:03,400
So if we send this link to our target, they'll be much more likely to click it because this is a legit

56
00:04:03,430 --> 00:04:04,990
website that they trust.

57
00:04:05,140 --> 00:04:11,350
So obviously you would replace this to whatever website that you think your target trusts and uses.

58
00:04:11,380 --> 00:04:18,520
And our goal, we've already created a cloned website in this URL of this website.

59
00:04:19,070 --> 00:04:23,210
Therefore, what we can actually do is simply copy this.

60
00:04:25,800 --> 00:04:31,170
And we're going to type wsa1 dotcom, which is a legit domain that the target trusts.

61
00:04:31,170 --> 00:04:34,860
And we're simply going to add a dash and we're just going to say blog.

62
00:04:35,430 --> 00:04:40,860
Now you can add as many dashes as you want in here and you can type whatever you want depending on the

63
00:04:40,860 --> 00:04:41,500
target.

64
00:04:41,520 --> 00:04:45,900
I'm simply typing blog because this is actually a blog.

65
00:04:46,620 --> 00:04:53,550
Then we're going to put the at symbol followed by the actual URL that we want to take the target to.

66
00:04:54,270 --> 00:05:01,620
So right now, if you look at this, it actually looks much less suspicious than the previous one because

67
00:05:01,620 --> 00:05:07,770
right now it actually starts with the domain name that the target will see, followed by the word blog,

68
00:05:07,770 --> 00:05:11,010
which also is representative of this website.

69
00:05:11,010 --> 00:05:16,590
And the suspicious part is actually coming at the back after the at symbol.

70
00:05:17,040 --> 00:05:19,260
So let's test if this works.

71
00:05:19,260 --> 00:05:25,690
And first, I'm actually going to show you what happens if you load this in Firefox because Firefox

will actually show a warning, as you can see in here.

73

But if you click on yes, it will work and it will take you to that website.

74

So as you can see, we ended up at the fake website at the fake clone of my blog.

75

But if we actually go to Chrome or Chromium, you'll notice that we will not see a warning and will

76
00:05:49,050 --> 00:05:56,160
directly land at the cloned website that might contain our hook or ask the user to log in and so on.

77
00:05:56,430 --> 00:06:00,900
So this is actually very, very useful because let me just do it very quick.

Imagine you have a fake login page to Instagram.

00:06:13,260 --> 00:06:16,080
by the suspicious URL that we have.

81
00:06:16,110 --> 00:06:22,830

82
00:06:22,980 --> 00:06:28,980
So when we actually load this now the user is going to see that this is Instagram.com and it's the login

83
00:06:28,980 --> 00:06:30,150
page of Instagram.

84
00:06:30,150 --> 00:06:32,550
So they are much more likely to click it.

85
00:06:32,550 --> 00:06:38,770
And if we load this on Chrome or chromium, as you can see, we're actually going to end up with the

86
00:06:38,770 --> 00:06:39,940
cloned website.

87
00:06:39,940 --> 00:06:45,790
So this could be a simple normal login page that actually doesn't steal anything, but instead you can

88
00:06:45,790 --> 00:06:50,710
have embedded in it your hook code and then they get hooked to beef.

89
00:06:50,710 --> 00:06:55,840
And we all know all of the cool stuff that you can do once a target is hooked to beef.

90

Now, you could actually argue that this bit right here is still a little bit suspicious.

91
00:07:02,080 --> 00:07:05,770
So Instagram.com dash login actually looks fine.

92
00:07:05,770 --> 00:07:09,880
This suspicious part is what we see in here after the Add symbol.

93
00:07:10,120 --> 00:07:12,520
And you might be right in thinking that.

94
00:07:12,520 --> 00:07:16,990
So let me show you how we can make this even less suspicious.

95
00:07:17,020 --> 00:07:19,450
We're going to go to this website right here.

96

I'm going to include it in the resources.

And basically what it does is you give it a domain name, for example, tiny URL, dot com.

98

I'm typing tiny URL because that is what we have here after the add symbol and what this website will

99

100
00:07:41,560 --> 00:07:43,690
So it's going to look a little bit different.

101
00:07:43,720 --> 00:07:45,280
Let me show you what it looks like.

102
00:07:45,280 --> 00:07:53,050
So if we click on Generate, you'll see that now tiny URL domain is actually transformed into this.

103
00:07:53,050 --> 00:07:59,690
So if I copy this and simply just paste it in my URL bar, you'll see I'll end up at tiny URL.

104
00:08:00,330 --> 00:08:06,720
So you could actually use this service on its own to make your domains look less suspicious.

105
00:08:06,720 --> 00:08:11,670
But what we're going to do in here, we're actually going to chain it with the method that we just learned

106
00:08:11,670 --> 00:08:13,080
in here with the dashes.

107
00:08:13,320 --> 00:08:19,110
So what we're going to do, we already typed on URL and I'm actually going to toggle the insert weird

108

characters that will make it look even more strange and harder to read.

109
00:08:23,520 --> 00:08:27,180
So this is the URL that we have now for tiny URL.

110
00:08:27,190 --> 00:08:34,620
I'm going to copy it and I'm going to paste it in here instead of the word Tinyurl.com.

00:08:35,710 --> 00:08:38,350
And now our domain looks like this.

112

Now, you might argue that this looks a little bit more suspicious than the previous one, but in my

114
00:08:49,630 --> 00:08:51,580
We have the blog, which is fine.

115
00:08:51,580 --> 00:08:57,250
And then after it, we have some strange characters followed by Zaid's blog again, which is fine again.

116

So the only part that is slightly suspicious is this part.

And you could easily think that this is some kind of an encryption used within the website that you

118

already trust, which is a.com or instagram.com.

00:09:10,990 --> 00:09:14,380

And perfect.

Again, as you can see, we landed at our cloned page, which could contain code, which could ask the

122

00:09:28,920 --> 00:09:32,760
or gain access to their system or steal usernames and passwords.

These are different tools that you could use with any of the other methods that you learned already.

127
00:09:50,370 --> 00:09:50,910

1

00:00:00,940  -->  00:00:06,580
So far we've seen how to social engineer our target to get them to click on a link

2

00:00:06,880  -->  00:00:11,800
and we did this by pretending to be a friend or a company that the target uses

3

00:00:12,020  -->  00:00:17,780
to click on a link.

4

00:00:17,780  -->  00:00:23,860
Google Analytics is a service provided by Google to websites and basically the service allows

5

00:00:24,720  -->  00:00:31,550
Now analytics can be used with websites, but it's also used with YouTube accounts

6

00:00:31,550  -->  00:00:32,740
and many other services.

7

00:00:33,940  -->  00:00:37,620
and some statistics released in 2017, suggested that more than 80% of websites use it.

8

00:00:37,620  -->  00:00:44,700
Now this is a bit bold, but I can tell you that it's used a lot.

9

00:00:44,700  -->  00:00:50,640
So, the example that we're going to do today we're going to do it on a YouTube account,

10

00:00:51,620  -->  00:00:57,620
and we're going to try to get our target to go to our beef web page and again you can use it with

11

00:00:57,620  -->  00:00:59,220
any other link you want.

12

00:01:00,800  -->  00:01:03,600
So, the first thing that we're gonna do is go to our target and that is gonna be my YouTube channel.

13

00:01:03,600  -->  00:01:08,500
This all depends on your on the information that you gathered, but your were assuming that

14

00:01:08,640  -->  00:01:16,120
when you were gathering information you managed to get a connection to this YouTube channel

15

00:01:16,360  -->  00:01:20,860
in here we're gonna see the code for this page and if the page uses Google Analytics,

16

00:01:21,720  -->  00:01:25,420
and as you can see we have the JavaScript code here that is given to our target by Google

17

00:01:25,520  -->  00:01:28,720
and in here you want to look for the tracking ID for this specific page

18

00:01:29,080  -->  00:01:35,300
and if you scroll to the right you'll see that we have a code here.

19

00:01:35,300  -->  00:01:36,500
I'm going to copy this and then I'm gonna go to my terminal

20

00:01:36,500  -->  00:01:41,460
and I'm gonna use set toolkit to launch this attack.

21

00:01:41,560  -->  00:01:46,980
I'm gonna go to social engineering attacks I'm gonna go to third-party modules number 11 in here

22

00:01:48,720  -->  00:01:55,360
and the only option we have here is number two which is Google Analytics attack

23

00:01:56,660  -->  00:02:00,340
and right now it gives you two options an automatic option and a manual option.

24

00:02:00,460  -->  00:02:03,020
or his friend or his company can be your target.

25

00:02:03,220  -->  00:02:07,780
This all depends on your on the information that you gathered, but your were assuming that

26

00:02:08,140  -->  00:02:13,880
when you were gathering information you managed to get a connection to this YouTube channel

27

00:02:13,880  -->  00:02:18,460
and you thought that this is a good idea for you to try and exploit your target.

28

00:02:19,180  -->  00:02:23,060
So we have the channel here or the website if you are doing this on a website.

29

00:02:23,380  -->  00:02:26,700
We're going to right click and view page source,

30

00:02:28,440  -->  00:02:34,300
in here we're gonna see the code for this page and if the page uses Google Analytics,

31

00:02:34,620  -->  00:02:40,860
it'll have its JavaScript or PHP code and you can find that by looking for analytics.

32

00:02:41,120  -->  00:02:44,300
So, I'm going to do Ctrl+F and look for analytics

33

00:02:44,820  -->  00:02:50,380
and as you can see we have the JavaScript code here that is given to our target by Google

34

00:02:51,340  -->  00:02:56,580
and in here you want to look for the tracking ID for this specific page

35

00:02:57,240  -->  00:03:02,240
and if you scroll to the right you'll see that we have a code here.

36

00:03:02,960  -->  00:03:07,380
Now if this was on a website, this would start with UA,

37

00:03:07,700  -->  00:03:11,560
but because it's on a YouTube channel it starts with YT.

38

00:03:12,160  -->  00:03:16,240
It doesn't really matter what it starts with we're interested into everything

39

00:03:16,240  -->  00:03:18,260
that comes after the first two letters.

40

00:03:18,920  -->  00:03:23,200
So, I'm copying everything after the first dash and up to the end

41

00:03:24,700  -->  00:03:29,400
I'm going to copy this and then I'm gonna go to my terminal

42

00:03:29,700  -->  00:03:33,240
and I'm gonna use set toolkit to launch this attack.

43

00:03:35,360  -->  00:03:41,820
I'm gonna go to social engineering attacks I'm gonna go to third-party modules number 11 in here

44

00:03:44,720  -->  00:03:49,920
and the only option we have here is number two which is Google Analytics attack

45

00:03:51,540  -->  00:03:56,000
and right now it gives you two options an automatic option and a manual option.

46

00:03:56,680  -->  00:04:01,460
The automatic option will just ask you for the URL of the target website

47

00:04:01,820  -->  00:04:05,720
and then it will automatically parse and get all the information that it needs.

48

00:04:05,900  -->  00:04:08,620
You won't need to view the page source like I did,

49

00:04:09,160  -->  00:04:13,940
but it doesn't really work with all websites and it definitely doesn't work with YouTube channels.

50

00:04:14,280  -->  00:04:17,960
So that's why I'm showing you how to do it using the manual way.

51

00:04:18,380  -->  00:04:19,760
So I'm going to type in manual

52

00:04:23,100  -->  00:04:27,680
and then it's going to ask us for the tracking ID and this is what we just copied,

53

00:04:28,080  -->  00:04:31,760
but like I told you we copied everything after the dash.

54

00:04:32,320  -->  00:04:36,860
So, I'm gonna add the URL manually, so I'm gonna type in URL myself

55

00:04:37,300  -->  00:04:39,920
and then I'm gonna paste the stuff that I copied

56

00:04:41,960  -->  00:04:47,160
I'm going to hit Enter, then it's asking me for the target host

57

00:04:47,680  -->  00:04:52,780
and this is the domain name for your target so again if you're targeting a website for example,

58

00:04:52,780  -->  00:04:56,440
if it was Google, then you type in https://google.com.

00:04:56,920  -->  00:05:02,280
My target is a YouTube channel so my host name is https://youtube.com,

60

00:05:03,220  -->  00:05:07,440
then it's asking us to everything that comes in after the host name.

00:05:07,920  -->  00:05:12,400
So this is everything that comes in after the first forward slash in here.

00:05:13,560  -->  00:05:15,260
So I'm gonna copy all of this,

hit Enter

64

00:05:22,600  -->  00:05:26,320
and then it's asking us for the page title I'm just going to put YouTube

65

00:05:28,380  -->  00:05:35,620
and then this is the most important part this is when it's asking you for the referral page.

66

00:05:35,860  -->  00:05:39,640
This is the page that you want the target to go to.

67

00:05:41,300  -->  00:05:48,840
So we want the target to go to our BeEF page which is working at http://10.20.14.213,

68

00:05:48,840  -->  00:05:50,580
so that's what I'm gonna put in here

and in there I have my BeEF page which looks exactly like my blog.

So, I'm gonna hit Enter

and that's it now telling us that the payload is ready

72

and what this payload it's gonna do is gonna it's gonna generate a lot of traffic to the target.

00:06:12,480  -->  00:06:19,200
So like I said Google Analytics is used to track the people that visit the website or the YouTube channel

74

00:06:19,440  -->  00:06:25,860

75

00:06:25,860  -->  00:06:29,520
that's going to visit this page, which is managed by your target

76

00:06:30,040  -->  00:06:36,480
and it's gonna show that the traffic is coming from 10.20.14.213,

77

00:06:36,480  -->  00:06:40,960
so it's coming from the link that you want your target to go to.

78

00:06:41,480  -->  00:06:45,300
Now once the target sees that they're getting a lot of traffic from a certain website.

79

80

00:06:52,040  -->  00:06:56,160

84

00:07:13,060  -->  00:07:16,000
and then the target person will be interested about that traffic.

85

00:07:16,400  -->  00:07:21,500
So I'm gonna say yes send it in a loop and then it's gift, it's asking us for the seconds

86

00:07:21,500  -->  00:07:24,880
to wait between the send requests.

87

00:07:24,880  -->  00:07:29,880
Now if you set a very short time you might be flagging some security firewalls.

88

00:07:30,080  -->  00:07:34,540
So, I'd set it I'm gonna set it for five because I'm not gonna let it run for a long time

89

00:07:34,760  -->  00:07:37,680
what I'd say is set a longer time than that.

90

00:07:38,540  -->  00:07:40,500
So, I'm gonna he'll answer to start it

91

00:07:41,000  -->  00:07:45,420
and this is going to send a request to our target every five seconds.

92

00:07:48,580  -->  00:07:50,800
Now, I'm gonna Ctrl+C this to stop it,

93

00:07:51,100  -->  00:07:57,100
but in a real-life situation you want to keep this running to generate a lot of traffic to your target.

94

00:07:57,980  -->  00:08:03,340
Now, we're going to assume that the website admin or the YouTube channel admin

96

00:08:09,840  -->  00:08:15,480
and if you use the large number, we're gonna see a large number of traffic coming to our website

or to our Channel.

100

00:08:30,100  -->  00:08:39,920
Now in a real life scenario you can upload the BeEF page to a free hosting and then use a domain name,

101

but it doesn't really work with

00:00:01,720  -->  00:00:05,040

00:00:05,340  -->  00:00:10,100

00:00:13,840  -->  00:00:20,040

5

00:00:20,320  -->  00:00:25,320

6

00:00:25,540  -->  00:00:29,700

00:00:29,700  -->  00:00:33,080
for example, if you're trying to get username and password for Facebook,

00:00:33,420  -->  00:00:37,300

9

00:00:37,300  -->  00:00:41,961
because you're-- what you're doing is you're actually just showing a fake Facebook page,

10

00:00:41,961  -->  00:00:45,420

11

00:00:46,520  -->  00:00:48,100
So, let's just click on this.

00:00:49,320  -->  00:00:54,660

00:00:55,360  -->  00:01:01,260

you'll see that they're being told that they got logged out of their session.

17

00:01:12,200  -->  00:01:14,660

Hit Enter.

21

00:01:25,500  -->  00:01:27,280
And if we go back here.

22

00:01:29,820  -->  00:01:35,700

23

00:01:36,740  -->  00:01:39,760

00:01:39,940  -->  00:01:43,560

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1

00:00:00,760  -->  00:00:05,620
Now in this lecture, I wanna show you a social engineering command that we can use with beEF,

7

00:00:27,260  -->  00:00:31,460

00:00:31,720  -->  00:00:35,840

First of all, it's asking you for the location where the Clippy image is stored.

00:01:49,640  -->  00:01:53,440

00:01:53,660  -->  00:01:56,020

00:01:56,540  -->  00:01:59,020

Then you can set the text that's gonna appear to the target person

which is gonna say, 'your Firefox is out-of-date,

33

00:02:25,680  -->  00:02:27,420
and it's in evil-files

34

35

36

and this is the message that the target person is gonna see after they download

00:02:43,600  -->  00:02:47,220

38

00:02:48,200  -->  00:02:51,460
So the plugin is very simple, it's in Social Engineering/Clippy,

39

00:02:51,720  -->  00:02:55,460
we configured our configuration here, we set the IPs correctly

and we set the location where our evil-file is stored.
